190925.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎥᏝ ᎤᏚᎸᏗ ᏂᎦᎵᏍᏗᏍᎬ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎢᏴᏛᎭᏉ ᎤᏓᎵᏍᎪᎸᏙᏗᏱ, ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᏂᏓᏕᏘᏴᎮᎬ, ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎤᏂᎩᎬ ᎬᏗ ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎨᎶᎯ ᏥᎾᏛᏁᎮ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎨᏒ ᏥᏩᏴᎯᎮᎢ;
190925.english: Nor yet that he should offer himself often, as the high priest entereth into the holy place every year with blood of others;
190925.phonetic: A-le v-tla u-du-lv-di ni-ga-li-s-di-s-gv i-yu-s-di i-yv-dv-ha-quo u-da-li-s-go-lv-do-di-yi, na-s-gi-ya ni-da-de-ti-yv-he-gv, go-hu-s-di u-ni-gi-gv gv-di nu-gv-wi-yu-sv a-tsi-lv--ge-lo-hi tsi-na-dv-ne-he ga-lv-quo-di-yu ge-sv tsi-wa-yv-hi-he-i;

190926.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏰᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏱᏄᏍᏕᎢ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎠᏎ ᎢᏳᎩᎵᏲᏨᎯ ᏱᏂᎦᎵᏍᏗᏍᎨ ᎡᎶᎯ ᏧᏙᏢᏅ ᏅᏓᎬᏩᏓᎴᏅᏛ;  ᎪᎯᏍᎩᏂ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎠᏓᎵᏍᎪᎸᏗᏍᎬ ᎠᏍᎦᏂ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎲᏍᎬᎢ ᏌᏉ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏄᏓᏛᏁᎸ ᎤᎵᏍᏆᎸᏗ ᏕᎨᏌᏗᏒᎢ.
190926.english: For then must he often have suffered since the foundation of the world: but now once in the end of the world hath he appeared to put away sin by the sacrifice of himself.
190926.phonetic: I-yu-ye-no na-s-gi yi-nu-s-de-i hna-quo u-tsa-ti a-se i-yu-gi-li-yo-tsv-hi yi-ni-ga-li-s-di-s-ge e-lo-hi tsu-do-tlv-nv nv-da-gv-wa-da-le-nv-dv;  go-hi-s-gi-ni ge-sv u-wa-sv a-da-li-s-go-lv-di-s-gv a-s-ga-ni ge-sv a-hv-s-gv-i sa-quo gv-ni-ge-sv nu-da-dv-ne-lv u-li-s-qua-lv-di de-ge-sa-di-sv-i.

190927.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᏧᎦᎪᏔᏅᎯ ᏥᎩ ᏴᏫ ᏌᏉ ᎢᏧᏂᏲᎯᏍᏗᏱ, ᎣᏂᏃ ᏗᎫᎪᏙᏗ ᎢᎦ ᎨᏒᎢ;
190927.english: And as it is appointed unto men once to die, but after this the judgment:
190927.phonetic: A-le tsu-ga-go-ta-nv-hi tsi-gi yv-wi sa-quo i-tsu-ni-yo-hi-s-di-yi, o-ni-no di-gu-go-do-di i-ga ge-sv-i;

190928.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᏌᏉ ᎢᏯᎦᎵᏍᎪᎸᏔᏅᎯ ᎤᎩᏍᏗᏱ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏂᏍᎦᏅᏨᎢ; ᎾᏍᎩᏃ Ꮎ ᎬᏩᎦᏖᏃᎯ ᎨᏒ ᏔᎵᏁ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏂᏙᏛᎠᏛᏁᎵ ᏧᏍᏕᎸᏗᏱ, ᎠᏍᎦᏂ ᎠᎫᏴᏙᏗ ᎾᏰᎲᎾ
190928.english: So Christ was once offered to bear the sins of many; and unto them that look for him shall he appear the second time without sin unto salvation.
190928.phonetic: Na-s-gi-ya na-s-quo Ga-lo-ne-dv sa-quo i-ya-ga-li-s-go-lv-ta-nv-hi u-gi-s-di-yi u-ni-tsa-ti ge-sv u-ni-s-ga-nv-tsv-i; na-s-gi-no na gv-wa-ga-te-no-hi ge-sv ta-li-ne gv-ni-ge-sv ni-do-dv-a-dv-ne-li tsu-s-de-lv-di-yi, a-s-ga-ni a-gu-yv-do-di na-ye-hv-na

191001.cherokee: ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗᏰᏃ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎤᎵᏰᎢᎶᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏓᏩᏗᏍᏙᏛ ᏧᏪᎲᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎧᎵᎢ ᏗᏟᎶᏍᏔᏅᎯ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᏥᎨᏒᎩ, ᎥᏝ ᏰᎵᎦ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᏓᏍᎪᎸᏙᏗ ᏓᏅᏗᏍᎬ, ᏂᏓᏕᏘᏴᎯᏒ ᏂᎪᎯᎸ ᏥᏓᎾᎵᏍᎪᎸᏗᏍᎨᎢ ᎾᎿᎭᎠᏂᎷᎩ ᏄᏂᎪᎸᎾ ᎢᎬᏩᏅᏁᏗ ᏱᎨᏎᎢ.
191001.english: For the law having a shadow of good things to come, and not the very image of the things, can never with those sacrifices which they offered year by year continually make the comers thereunto perfect.
191001.phonetic: Di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di-ye-no o-s-dv u-li-ye-i-lo-hi-s-di ge-sv u-da-wa-di-s-do-dv tsu-we-hv-gi, a-le a-ka-li-i di-tli-lo-s-ta-nv-hi ni-ge-sv-na tsi-ge-sv-gi, v-tla ye-li-ga, na-s-gi di-da-s-go-lv-do-di da-nv-di-s-gv, ni-da-de-ti-yv-hi-sv ni-go-hi-lv tsi-da-na-li-s-go-lv-di-s-ge-i na-hna a-ni-lu-gi nu-ni-go-lv-na i-gv-wa-nv-ne-di yi-ge-se-i.

191002.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏰᏃ ᏰᎵᏉ ᏱᎨᏎᎢ, Ꮭ ᏱᎬᏩᏂᏲᎯᏍᏔᏁ ᏧᎾᎵᏍᎪᎸᏗᏍᎬᎢ; ᎠᎾᎵᏍᎪᎸᏗᏍᎩᏰᏃ ᎨᏒ ᏌᏉ ᏱᏄᎾᏓᏅᎦᎸᎮ ᎥᏝ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᎾ ᏱᎬᏩᏂᏰᎸᏎᎢ.
191002.english: For then would they not have ceased to be offered? because that the worshippers once purged should have had no more conscience of sins.
191002.phonetic: I-yu-ye-no ye-li-quo yi-ge-se-i, tla yi-gv-wa-ni-yo-hi-s-ta-ne tsu-na-li-s-go-lv-di-s-gv-i; a-na-li-s-go-lv-di-s-gi-ye-no ge-sv sa-quo yi-nu-na-da-nv-ga-lv-he v-tla hna-quo a-ni-s-ga-na yi-gv-wa-ni-ye-lv-se-i.

191003.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏍᎩᏂ Ꮎ ᏓᎾᎵᏍᎪᎸᏗᏍᎬᎢ, ᏂᏓᏕᏘᏴᎯᏒ ᎤᎾᏅᏓᏗᏏᏐᏗᏉ ᏂᎦᎵᏍᏗᏍᎪ ᎤᏂᏍᎦᏅᏨᎢ.
191003.english: But in those sacrifices there is a remembrance again made of sins every year.
191003.phonetic: Na-s-gi-s-gi-ni na da-na-li-s-go-lv-di-s-gv-i, ni-da-de-ti-yv-hi-sv u-na-nv-da-di-si-so-di-quo ni-ga-li-s-di-s-go u-ni-s-ga-nv-tsv-i.

191004.cherokee: ᎥᏝᏰᏃ ᏩᎦ ᏧᎾᎪᏅᏍᏓᎵ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏫ ᏗᏂᎭᏄᎸᎯ ᎤᏂᎩᎬ ᏰᎵ ᎬᏩᏂᎲᏍᏗ ᏱᎨᏎ ᎠᏍᎦᏂ ᎨᏒᎢ.
191004.english: For it is not possible that the blood of bulls and of goats should take away sins.
191004.phonetic: V-tla-ye-no wa-ga tsu-na-go-nv-s-da-li a-le a-wi di-ni-ha-nu-lv-hi u-ni-gi-gv ye-li gv-wa-ni-hv-s-di yi-ge-se a-s-ga-ni ge-sv-i.

191005.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ (ᎦᎶᏁᏛ) ᎡᎶᎯ ᎤᎾᏄᎪᏥᎸ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ, ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎨᎳᏍᏙᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎢᎵᏍᎪᎸᏙᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎥᏝ ᏰᎵ ᏱᏗᏣᏰᎸᏁᎢ, ᎠᏰᎸᏍᎩᏂ ᏍᏆᏛᏅᎢᏍᏓᏁᎸ.
191005.english: Wherefore when he cometh into the world, he saith, Sacrifice and offering thou wouldest not, but a body hast thou prepared me:
191005.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di (Ga-lo-ne-dv) e-lo-hi u-na-nu-go-tsi-lv, hi-a nu-we-se-i, A-tsi-lv--ge-la-s-do-di a-le i-li-s-go-lv-do-di ge-sv v-tla ye-li yi-di-tsa-ye-lv-ne-i, a-ye-lv-s-gi-ni s-qua-dv-nv-i-s-da-ne-lv.

191006.cherokee: ᏧᏃᏍᏛ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᏗᎨᎳᏍᏙᏗ ᎠᎴ ᏗᎵᏍᎪᎸᏙᏗ ᎠᏍᎦᏂ ᎠᎫᏴᏙᏗ ᎥᏝ ᎣᏏᏳ ᏱᏗᏣᏰᎸᏁᎢ;
191006.english: In burnt offerings and sacrifices for sin thou hast had no pleasure.
191006.phonetic: Tsu-no-s-dv a-tsi-lv--di-ge-la-s-do-di a-le di-li-s-go-lv-do-di a-s-ga-ni a-gu-yv-do-di v-tla o-si-yu yi-di-tsa-ye-lv-ne-i;

191007.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎯᎠ ᎾᎩᏪᏒᎩ, ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᏥᎷᎩ, [ᎪᏪᎵᎯ ᎪᏪᎳ ᎥᎩᏃᎮᏍᎬᎢ,] [ᏥᎷᎩ] ᏄᏍᏛ ᏣᏚᎵᏍᎬ ᎢᏯᏆᏛᏁᏗ Ᏹ, ᏣᏁᎳᏅᎯ.
191007.english: Then said I, Lo, I come (in the volume of the book it is written of me,) to do thy will, O God.
191007.phonetic: Hna-quo-no hi-a na-gi-we-sv-gi, Gv-ni-yu-quo tsi-lu-gi, [go-we-li-hi go-we-la v-gi-no-he-s-gv-i,] [tsi-lu-gi] nu-s-dv tsa-du-li-s-gv i-ya-qua-dv-ne-di yi, Tsa-ne-la-nv-hi.

191008.cherokee: ᎦᏳᎳ ᎯᎠ ᎢᏳᏪᏛ ᏥᎩ, ᎠᏥᎸᎨᎳᏍᏙᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎠᎵᏍᎪᎸᏙᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᏃᏍᏗ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᏗᎨᎳᏍᏙᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᎵᏍᎪᎸᏙᏗ ᎠᏍᎦᏂ ᎠᎫᏴᏙᏗ, ᎥᏝ ᏰᎵ ᏱᏗᏣᏰᎸᏁᎢ, ᎥᏝ ᎠᎴ ᎣᏏᏳ ᏱᏗᏣᏰᎸᏁᎢ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᏂᎬᏅ ᏗᎵᏍᎪᎸᏔᏅᎯ ᏥᎨᏎᎢ;
191008.english: Above when he said, Sacrifice and offering and burnt offerings and offering for sin thou wouldest not, neither hadst pleasure therein; which are offered by the law;
191008.phonetic: Ga-yu-la hi-a i-yu-we-dv tsi-gi, A-tsi-lv-ge-la-s-do-di a-le a-li-s-go-lv-do-di, a-le tsu-no-s-di a-tsi-lv--di-ge-la-s-do-di, a-le di-li-s-go-lv-do-di a-s-ga-ni a-gu-yv-do-di, v-tla ye-li yi-di-tsa-ye-lv-ne-i, v-tla a-le o-si-yu yi-di-tsa-ye-lv-ne-i; na-s-gi di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di na-s-gi-ya ni-gv-nv di-li-s-go-lv-ta-nv-hi tsi-ge-se-i;

191009.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ, ᎬᏂᏳᏉ, ᏥᎷᎩ ᏄᏍᏛ ᏣᏚᎵᏍᎬ ᎢᏯᏆᏛᏁᏗᏱ ᏣᏁᎳᏅᎯ.  ᎢᎬᏱᏱ [ᎤᏁᎢᏍᏔᏅ] ᎠᏲᏍᏗᎭ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏔᎵᏁ [ᎤᏁᎢᏍᏔᏅ] ᎤᏍᏓᏱᏗᏍᏗᏱ.
191009.english: Then said he, Lo, I come to do thy will, O God. He taketh away the first, that he may establish the second.
191009.phonetic: Hna-quo hi-a nu-we-se-i, Gv-ni-yu-quo, tsi-lu-gi nu-s-dv tsa-du-li-s-gv i-ya-qua-dv-ne-di-yi Tsa-ne-la-nv-hi.  I-gv-yi-yi [u-ne-i-s-ta-nv] a-yo-s-di-ha, na-s-gi ta-li-ne [u-ne-i-s-ta-nv] u-s-da-yi-di-s-di-yi.

191010.cherokee: [ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯᏰᏃ] ᎣᏏᏳ ᎤᏰᎸᏗ ᎡᎩᏅᎦᎸᏗᏱ ᏥᏌ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᎠᏰᎸ ᏌᏉ ᎢᏯᎵᏍᎪᎸᏔᏅᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏳᏩᏂᏌᏛ.
191010.english: By the which will we are sanctified through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ once for all.
191010.phonetic: [U-ne-la-nv-hi-ye-no] o-si-yu u-ye-lv-di e-gi-nv-ga-lv-di-yi Tsi-sa Ga-lo-ne-dv a-ye-lv sa-quo i-ya-li-s-go-lv-ta-nv-hi ge-sv i-yu-wa-ni-sa-dv.

191011.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎾᏂᎥ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎠᏁᎶᎯ ᏓᏂᏙᎨ ᏚᏂᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎮ ᏂᏓᏙᏓᏈᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏂᎪᎯᎸ ᎤᏠᏱᏉ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᏗᎨᎳᏍᏙᏗ ᏧᎾᎵᏍᎪᎸᏗᏍᎨᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏍᎦᏂ ᎨᏒ ᎢᎸᎯᏳ ᏰᎵ ᎬᏩᎲᏍᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ.
191011.english: And every priest standeth daily ministering and offering oftentimes the same sacrifices, which can never take away sins:
191011.phonetic: A-le na-ni-v a-tsi-lv--a-ne-lo-hi da-ni-do-ge du-ni-lv-wi-s-da-ne-he ni-da-do-da-qui-sv-i, a-le ni-go-hi-lv u-tlo-yi-quo a-tsi-lv--di-ge-la-s-do-di tsu-na-li-s-go-lv-di-s-ge-i, na-s-gi a-s-ga-ni ge-sv i-lv-hi-yu ye-li gv-wa-hv-s-di ni-ge-sv-na ge-sv-i.

191012.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏍᎩᏂ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᏌᏉ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᎪᎸᏔᏅᎯ ᎨᏎ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎨᎳᏍᏙᏗ ᎠᏍᎦᏂ ᎠᎫᎪᏙᏗ, ᎤᏪᏁ ᎤᎵᏍᏆᏗᏍᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎠᎦᏗᏏᏗᏢ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏬᎸᎢ;
191012.english: But this man, after he had offered one sacrifice for sins for ever, sat down on the right hand of God;
191012.phonetic: Hi-a-s-gi-ni a-s-ga-ya sa-quo i-yu-li-s-go-lv-ta-nv-hi ge-se a-tsi-lv--ge-la-s-do-di a-s-ga-ni a-gu-go-do-di, u-we-ne u-li-s-qua-di-s-di ni-ge-sv-na a-ga-di-si-di-tlv U-ne-la-nv-hi u-wo-lv-i;

191013.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎿᎭᏉ ᏂᎪᎯᎸ ᎠᎦᏘᏯ ᎬᏂ ᎬᏩᏍᎦᎩ ᏧᎳᏏᏗᏱ ᎦᏍᎩᎶ ᏂᎨᎬᏁᎸᎭ.
191013.english: From henceforth expecting till his enemies be made his footstool.
191013.phonetic: A-le hna-quo ni-go-hi-lv a-ga-ti-ya gv-ni gv-wa-s-ga-gi tsu-la-si-di-yi ga-s-gi-lo ni-ge-gv-ne-lv-ha.

191014.cherokee: ᏌᏉᏰᏃ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᎪᎸᏔᏅᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏮᏔᏅ ᎤᎵᏍᏆᏗᏍᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᏄᏂᎪᎸᎾ ᏂᏚᏩᏁᎸ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎾᏂᏍᎦᏅᎾ ᎢᎨᎬᏁᎸᎯ.
191014.english: For by one offering he hath perfected for ever them that are sanctified.
191014.phonetic: Sa-quo-ye-no i-yu-li-s-go-lv-ta-nv-hi ge-sv u-wv-ta-nv u-li-s-qua-di-s-di ni-ge-sv-na nu-ni-go-lv-na ni-du-wa-ne-lv na-s-gi na na-ni-s-ga-nv-na i-ge-gv-ne-lv-hi.

191015.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎦᎸᏉᏗ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᎣᎪᎯᏳᏓᏁᎯ; ᎦᏳᎳᏰᏃ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏪᏛ ᏂᎨᏐᎢ,
191015.english: Whereof the Holy Ghost also is a witness to us: for after that he had said before,
191015.phonetic: A-le na-s-quo Ga-lv-quo-di A-da-nv-do o-go-hi-yu-da-ne-hi; ga-yu-la-ye-no hi-a na-s-gi i-yu-we-dv ni-ge-so-i,

191016.cherokee: ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᏍᏕᏍᏗ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᏙᏓᎦᏥᏯᏠᎢᏍᏓᏁᎵ ᎾᎯᏳ ᎦᎶᏐᏅᎭ ᎠᏗᎭ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ; ᏗᏆᏤᎵ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᏧᏂᎾᏫᏱ ᏙᏓᏥᎳᏂ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᎾᏓᏅᏛ ᏙᏓᎪᏪᎳᏂ.
191016.english: This is the covenant that I will make with them after those days, saith the Lord, I will put my laws into their hearts, and in their minds will I write them;
191016.phonetic: Hi-a na-s-gi nu-s-de-s-di ka-no-he-dv do-da-ga-tsi-ya-tlo-i-s-da-ne-li na-hi-yu ga-lo-so-nv-ha a-di-ha U-gv-wi-yu-hi; Di-qua-tse-li di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di tsu-ni-na-wi-yi do-da-tsi-la-ni, a-le du-na-da-nv-dv do-da-go-we-la-ni.

191017.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏍᎦᏅᏨ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏲ ᏄᎾᏛᏁᎵᏙᎸ ᎥᏝ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎠᏆᏅᏓᏗᏍᏗ ᏱᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
191017.english: And their sins and iniquities will I remember no more.
191017.phonetic: A-le u-ni-s-ga-nv-tsv a-le u-yo nu-na-dv-ne-li-do-lv v-tla hna-quo a-qua-nv-da-di-s-di yi-ge-se-s-di.

191018.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏍᎦᏅᏨᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎦᏴᏓᏗᏁᎸᎯ ᏱᎩ, ᎥᏝ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎠᏍᎦᏂ ᎠᎫᏴᏙᏗ ᎠᎵᏍᎪᎸᏙᏗ ᏱᏂᎦᎵᏍᏗᎭ.
191018.english: Now where remission of these is, there is no more offering for sin.
191018.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no hi-a na-s-gi a-s-ga-nv-tsv-hi ge-sv ga-yv-da-di-ne-lv-hi yi-gi, v-tla hna-quo a-s-ga-ni a-gu-yv-do-di a-li-s-go-lv-do-di yi-ni-ga-li-s-di-ha.

191019.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ, ᎢᏓᎵᏅᏟ, ᏂᏗᎾᏰᏍᎬᎾ ᏫᎨᎩᏴᏍᏗ ᏥᏄᎵᏍᏔᎤ ᎤᏟ ᎢᎦᎸᏉᏗᎨᏒ ᏥᏌ ᎤᎩᎬ ᎢᏳᏩᏂᏌᏅᎯ.
191019.english: Having therefore, brethren, boldness to enter into the holiest by the blood of Jesus,
191019.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di, i-da-li-nv-tli, ni-di-na-ye-s-gv-na wi-ge-gi-yv-s-di tsi-nu-li-s-ta-u u-tli i-ga-lv-quo-di-ge-sv Tsi-sa u-gi-gv i-yu-wa-ni-sa-nv-hi.

191020.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎢᏤ ᎠᎴ ᎬᏂᏛ ᎠᏓᏁᎯ ᎦᏅᏅ ᎬᏔᏅᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᎩᏍᏓᏱᏕᎸᎯ ᏥᎩ ᎠᏰᏙᎳᏛ ᎤᎶᏒᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏇᏓᎸ ᏥᎦᏛᎦ;
191020.english: By a new and living way, which he hath consecrated for us, through the veil, that is to say, his flesh;
191020.phonetic: Na-s-gi na i-tse a-le gv-ni-dv a-da-ne-hi ga-nv-nv gv-ta-nv-hi, na-s-gi i-gi-s-da-yi-de-lv-hi tsi-gi a-ye-do-la-dv u-lo-sv-i, na-s-gi u-que-da-lv tsi-ga-dv-ga;

191021.cherokee: ᎠᎴ [ᏥᎩᎧᎭ] ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏌᏕᎩ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎨᎶᎯ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵ ᎠᏓᏁᎸ ᎤᎦᏌᏯᏍᏗᏕᎩ;
191021.english: And having an high priest over the house of God;
191021.phonetic: A-le [tsi-gi-ka-ha] nu-gv-wi-yu-sa-de-gi a-tsi-lv--ge-lo-hi U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li a-da-ne-lv u-ga-sa-ya-s-di-de-gi;

191022.cherokee: ᎾᎥ ᎢᏗᎷᎨᏍᏗ ᎢᏛᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᏄᏠᎾᏍᏛᏅ ᏗᎩᎾᏫ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎵᏂᎩᏛ ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᎩᎾᏫ ᏤᎩᏍᏚᏞᎸᎯ ᏤᎩᏅᎦᎸᎯ ᎤᏲ ᎠᏓᏅᏖᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᏗᏰᎸ ᎦᏓᎭ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎠᎹ ᏤᎪᏑᎴᏓᏁᎸᎯ.
191022.english: Let us draw near with a true heart in full assurance of faith, having our hearts sprinkled from an evil conscience, and our bodies washed with pure water.
191022.phonetic: Na-v i-di-lu-ge-s-di i-dv-di-s-ge-s-di nu-tlo-na-s-dv-nv di-gi-na-wi, a-le u-li-ni-gi-dv go-hi-yu-di ge-sv-i, a-le di-gi-na-wi tse-gi-s-du-tle-lv-hi tse-gi-nv-ga-lv-hi u-yo a-da-nv-te-di ge-sv-i, a-le di-di-ye-lv ga-da-ha ni-ge-sv-na a-ma tse-go-su-le-da-ne-lv-hi.

191023.cherokee: ᎠᏍᏓᏯ ᏕᎩᏂᏴᏎᏍᏗ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏂᎬᏁᎸ ᎤᏚᎩ ᎢᎬᏒ ᏂᎦᏜᏓᏏᏛᎡᎲᎾ; ᏄᏓᎵᏓᏍᏛᎾᏰᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎤᏚᎢᏍᏔᏅᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ.
191023.english: Let us hold fast the profession of our faith without wavering; (for he is faithful that promised;)
191023.phonetic: A-s-da-ya de-gi-ni-yv-se-s-di gv-ni-ge-sv ni-gv-ne-lv u-du-gi i-gv-sv ni-ga-dla-si-dv-e-hv-na; nu-da-li-da-s-dv-na-ye-no na-s-gi na u-du-i-s-ta-nv-hi ge-sv-i.

191024.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᏕᏓᏓᏓᏅᏖᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎠᏓᎨᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎴ ᏱᏍᏛ ᏗᎦᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏗᎦᏓᏂᎳᏕᏗᏱ ᏗᎩᏄᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗᏱ;
191024.english: And let us consider one another to provoke unto love and to good works:
191024.phonetic: A-le de-da-da-da-nv-te-s-ge-s-di, a-da-ge-yu-di ge-sv a-le yi-s-dv di-ga-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di ge-sv di-ga-da-ni-la-de-di-yi di-gi-nu-wi-s-da-ne-di-yi;

191025.cherokee: ᏞᏍᏗᏉ ᏱᏗᏑᎵᎪᎨᏍᏗ ᎢᏓᏓᏟᏌᏂᏙᎲᎢ, ᎢᎦᏛ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᎾᏛᏁᏗ ᏥᎩ; ᏕᏣᏓᏬᏁᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗᏍᎩᏂ; ᎠᎴ Ꮀ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᏎᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏂᏣᏛᏁᎮᏍᏗ ᏥᏥᎪᏩᏗ ᎤᏍᏆᎸᎯᏗᏒ ᎾᎯᏳ ᎢᎦ.
191025.english: Not forsaking the assembling of ourselves together, as the manner of some is; but exhorting one another: and so much the more, as ye see the day approaching.
191025.phonetic: Tle-s-di-quo yi-di-su-li-go-ge-s-di i-da-da-tli-sa-ni-do-hv-i, i-ga-dv na-s-gi i-yu-na-dv-ne-di tsi-gi; de-tsa-da-wo-ne-di-s-ge-s-di-s-gi-ni; a-le ho u-gv-wi-yu-se-s-di, na-s-gi ni-tsa-dv-ne-he-s-di tsi-tsi-go-wa-di u-s-qua-lv-hi-di-sv na-hi-yu i-ga.

191026.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏰᏃ ᎢᎬᏒ ᎢᎦᏚᎸᏛ ᏱᏗᏍᎦᏅᎬ ᏚᏳᎳ ᎨᎩᎦᏁᎥᏒᎯ ᏚᏳᎪᏛ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎥᏝ ᏅᏩᏓᎴ ᏯᎵᏃᎯᏯ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎨᎳᏍᏗ ᎠᏍᎦᏂ ᎠᎫᏴᏙᏗ,
191026.english: For if we sin wilfully after that we have received the knowledge of the truth, there remaineth no more sacrifice for sins,
191026.phonetic: I-yu-ye-no i-gv-sv i-ga-du-lv-dv yi-di-s-ga-nv-gv du-yu-la ge-gi-ga-ne-v-sv-hi du-yu-go-dv ge-sv-i, v-tla nv-wa-da-le ya-li-no-hi-ya a-tsi-lv--ge-la-s-di a-s-ga-ni a-gu-yv-do-di,

191027.cherokee: ᎤᎾᏰᎯᏍᏗᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᎣᏓᏅᏛ ᎠᎦᏖᎾᏍᏗᏱ ᏗᎫᎪᏙᏗ ᎢᎦ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏔᎳᏬᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᏥᎸ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏂᏛᏙᏗ ᏥᎩ ᎬᏩᏍᎦᎩ.
191027.english: But a certain fearful looking for of judgment and fiery indignation, which shall devour the adversaries.
191027.phonetic: U-na-ye-hi-s-di-quo-s-gi-ni o-da-nv-dv a-ga-te-na-s-di-yi di-gu-go-do-di i-ga ge-sv-i, a-le u-ta-la-wo-hi-s-di ge-sv a-tsi-lv na-s-gi-ya, na-s-gi u-ni-dv-do-di tsi-gi gv-wa-s-ga-gi.

191028.cherokee: ᎩᎶ ᎼᏏ ᎤᏤᎵ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎢᏳᏩᏁᏔᏅᎯ ᏥᎨᏎ ᎠᏲᎱᏍᎨ ᎦᏰᏥᏙᎵᏍᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎠᏂᏔᎵᎭ ᎠᏂᎦᏔᎯ ᎤᏂᏃᎮᎸᎯ ᎬᏔᏅᎯ;
191028.english: He that despised Moses' law died without mercy under two or three witnesses:
191028.phonetic: Gi-lo Mo-si u-tse-li di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di i-yv-dv i-yu-wa-ne-ta-nv-hi tsi-ge-se a-yo-hu-s-ge ga-ye-tsi-do-li-s-di ni-ge-sv-na a-ni-ta-li-ha a-ni-ga-ta-hi u-ni-no-he-lv-hi gv-ta-nv-hi;

191029.cherokee: ᏝᏍᎪ ᎤᏟ ᎢᎦᎢ ᏱᏂᏙᎬᏩᏳᎪᏗ ᎠᏥᏍᏛᏗᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᎩᎶ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎤᎳᏍᏓᎡᎸᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎩᎬ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᏗᏠᎯᏍᏙᏔᏅᎯ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏓᏅᎦᎶᏔᏅᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎦᏓᎭ ᎤᏰᎸᏅᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎦᎬᏩᏐᏢᏔᏅᎯ ᎬᏩᎦᏘᏯ ᎠᏓᏙᎵᎩ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ.
191029.english: Of how much sorer punishment, suppose ye, shall he be thought worthy, who hath trodden under foot the Son of God, and hath counted the blood of the covenant, wherewith he was sanctified, an unholy thing, and hath done despite unto the Spirit of grace?
191029.phonetic: Tla-s-go u-tli i-ga-i yi-ni-do-gv-wa-yu-go-di a-tsi-s-dv-di-s-do-di-yi gi-lo U-ne-la-nv-hi U-we-tsi u-la-s-da-e-lv-hi, a-le na-s-gi na gi-gv-ka-no-he-dv di-tlo-hi-s-do-ta-nv-hi na-s-gi u-da-nv-ga-lo-ta-nv-hi ge-sv ga-da-ha u-ye-lv-nv-hi, a-le ga-gv-wa-so-tlv-ta-nv-hi gv-wa-ga-ti-ya a-da-do-li-gi A-da-nv-do.

191030.cherokee: ᎡᏗᎦᏔᎭᏰᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎯᎠ ᎢᏳᏪᏛ ᏥᎩ, ᎠᏞᎢᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᏴ ᎠᏆᏤᎵᎦ, ᎠᏴ ᏓᎦᎫᏴᎯ, ᎠᏗᎭ ᏱᎰᏩ.  ᎠᎴ ᏔᎵᏁᎢ, ᏱᎰᏩ ᏙᏓᎫᎪᏓᏁᎵ ᏧᏤᎵ ᏴᏫ.
191030.english: For we know him that hath said, Vengeance belongeth unto me, I will recompense, saith the Lord. And again, The Lord shall judge his people.
191030.phonetic: E-di-ga-ta-ha-ye-no na-s-gi na hi-a i-yu-we-dv tsi-gi, A-tle-i-s-di ge-sv a-yv a-qua-tse-li-ga, a-yv da-ga-gu-yv-hi, a-di-ha Yi-ho-wa.  A-le ta-li-ne-i, Yi-ho-wa do-da-gu-go-da-ne-li tsu-tse-li yv-wi.

191031.english: It is a fearful thing to fall into the hands of the living God.

191032.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎢᏣᏅᏓᏓ ᏧᏩᎫᏔᏅᏒᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᎯᏳ ᎢᎦ ᏂᏕᏣᏗᏍᏓᏁᎶᎢ, ᎤᏣᏘ ᎢᏣᏟᏂᎬᏁᎸ ᏕᏥᎦᏘᎸᏒ ᎠᎩᎵᏲᎢᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ;
191032.english: But call to remembrance the former days, in which, after ye were illuminated, ye endured a great fight of afflictions;
191032.phonetic: A-se-no i-tsa-nv-da-da tsu-wa-gu-ta-nv-sv-i, na-s-gi na-hi-yu i-ga ni-de-tsa-di-s-da-ne-lo-i, u-tsa-ti i-tsa-tli-ni-gv-ne-lv de-tsi-ga-ti-lv-sv a-gi-li-yo-i-s-di ge-sv-i;

191033.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᎸᎯᏳ ᏥᎦᏰᏥᏐᏢᏛᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏤᏥᎩᎵᏲᎢᏍᏗᏍᎬᎢ; ᎢᎸᎯᏳᏃ ᏕᏣᎵᎪᏁᎸ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᎨᎬᏁᎸᎯ.
191033.english: Partly, whilst ye were made a gazingstock both by reproaches and afflictions; and partly, whilst ye became companions of them that were so used.
191033.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-lv-hi-yu tsi-ga-ye-tsi-so-tlv-dv-i, a-le tse-tsi-gi-li-yo-i-s-di-s-gv-i; i-lv-hi-yu-no de-tsa-li-go-ne-lv na-s-gi i-ge-gv-ne-lv-hi.

191034.cherokee: ᎥᏆᎸᎢᏛᏰᏃ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎥᏍᎩᏯᏙᎵᏨᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎵᎮᎵᏍᏗᏉ ᎥᏣᏓᏅᏛᎩ ᎡᏥᏬᏅᎲ ᏧᎬᏩᎶᏗ ᎢᏥᎲᎢ, ᎢᏥᎦᏔᎲᎩᏰᏃ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᏫᏥᏍᏆᏂᎪᏛ ᎤᏟ ᎢᏲᏍᏛ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏲᎩ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᏧᎬᏩᎶᏗ.
191034.english: For ye had compassion of me in my bonds, and took joyfully the spoiling of your goods, knowing in yourselves that ye have in heaven a better and an enduring substance.
191034.phonetic: V-qua-lv-i-dv-ye-no ge-sv i-yu-s-di v-s-gi-ya-do-li-tsv-gi, a-le u-li-he-li-s-di-quo v-tsa-da-nv-dv-gi e-tsi-wo-nv-hv tsu-gv-wa-lo-di i-tsi-hv-i, i-tsi-ga-ta-hv-gi-ye-no ga-lv-la-di wi-tsi-s-qua-ni-go-dv u-tli i-yo-s-dv a-le a-yo-gi ni-ge-sv-na tsu-gv-wa-lo-di.

191035.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏞᏍᏗ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎢᏨᏁᏔᏅᎩ ᎢᏦᎯᏳᏒᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎤᏓᎫᏴᎡᏗ ᏥᎩ.
191035.english: Cast not away therefore your confidence, which hath great recompence of reward.
191035.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di tle-s-di i-yv-dv i-tsv-ne-ta-nv-gi i-tso-hi-yu-sv-i, na-s-gi u-tsa-ti u-da-gu-yv-e-di tsi-gi.

191036.cherokee: ᏗᏨᏂᏗᏳ ᎢᏣᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᎤᏚᎸᏗᏳ ᏂᏣᎵᏍᏓᏁᎭ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏏᏳ ᎤᏴᎸᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏣᏛᏁᎸᎯ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎢᏣᏤᎵ ᎢᎬᏩᎵᏍᏙᏗ ᏱᎩ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎠᏚᎢᏍᏛᎢ.
191036.english: For ye have need of patience, that, after ye have done the will of God, ye might receive the promise.
191036.phonetic: Di-tsv-ni-di-yu i-tsa-li-s-do-di-yi u-du-lv-di-yu ni-tsa-li-s-da-ne-ha; na-s-gi hna-quo U-ne-la-nv-hi u-si-yu u-yv-lv-di ge-sv i-tsa-dv-ne-lv-hi ge-se-s-di, i-tsa-tse-li i-gv-wa-li-s-do-di yi-gi nu-s-dv a-du-i-s-dv-i.

191037.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏰᏃ ᏂᎪᎯᎸᎾᏉ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎷᎯᏍᏗ ᏥᎩ ᏓᎦᎷᏥ, ᎠᎴ ᎥᏝ ᏴᏛᏙᎯᏗ.
191037.english: For yet a little while, and he that shall come will come, and will not tarry.
191037.phonetic: Hna-quo-ye-no ni-go-hi-lv-na-quo, na-s-gi u-lu-hi-s-di tsi-gi da-ga-lu-tsi, a-le v-tla yv-dv-do-hi-di.

191038.cherokee: ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏘᏃ ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎾᏛᏂᏗᏍᏕᏍᏗ; ᎩᎶᏍᎩᏂ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎢᎠᏨᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎠᏆᏓᏅᏙ ᎥᏝ ᎣᏏᏳ ᏳᏰᎸᏎᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᎩ.
191038.english: Now the just shall live by faith: but if any man draw back, my soul shall have no pleasure in him.
191038.phonetic: U-na-da-nv-ti-no go-hi-yu-di ge-sv u-na-dv-ni-di-s-de-s-di; gi-lo-s-gi-ni i-yu-s-di i-a-tsv-s-ge-s-di a-qua-da-nv-do v-tla o-si-yu yu-ye-lv-se-s-di na-s-gi.

191039.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎥᏝ ᎠᎾᏨᏍᎩ ᏥᎩ [ᎤᎾᏓᏡᎬ] ᎢᏖᎳ ᏱᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎨᏥᏛᏙᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏥᏩᏂᎷᎦ, ᎾᏍᎩᏍᎩᏂ Ꮎ ᎤᎾᏓᏡᎬ ᎢᏖᎳ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏃᎯᏳᏒ ᏥᏓᏂᏍᏕᎸᏗ ᏧᎾᏓᏅᏙ.
191039.english: But we are not of them who draw back unto perdition; but of them that believe to the saving of the soul.
191039.phonetic: A-se-no v-tla a-na-tsv-s-gi tsi-gi [u-na-da-tlu-gv] i-te-la yi-gi, na-s-gi ge-tsi-dv-do-di ge-sv i-yv-dv tsi-wa-ni-lu-ga, na-s-gi-s-gi-ni na u-na-da-tlu-gv i-te-la, na-s-gi u-no-hi-yu-sv tsi-da-ni-s-de-lv-di tsu-na-da-nv-do.

191101.cherokee: ᎪᎯᏳᏗᏃ ᎨᏒ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏍᏗ ᎦᏛᎬᎢ, ᎤᎵᏂᎩᏛ ᎪᎯᏳᏗᏱ ᎠᎪᏩᏛᏗᏱ ᎨᏒ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎤᏚᎩ ᎣᏩᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏄᏜᏓᏏᏛᏒᎾ ᎢᏳᏓᎵᏍᏓᏁᏗᏱ ᎡᎲ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎠᏏ ᎠᎪᎲᎯ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ.
191101.english: Now faith is the substance of things hoped for, the evidence of things not seen.
191101.phonetic: Go-hi-yu-di-no ge-sv hi-a nu-s-di ga-dv-gv-i, u-li-ni-gi-dv go-hi-yu-di-yi a-go-wa-dv-di-yi ge-sv nu-s-dv u-du-gi o-wa-sv-i, a-le nu-dla-si-dv-sv-na i-yu-da-li-s-da-ne-di-yi e-hv na-s-gi na a-si a-go-hv-hi ni-ge-sv-na ge-sv-i.

191102.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏰᏃ ᎠᏅᏗᏍᎬ ᎡᏘ ᎤᎾᏕᏅᎯ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎨᏥᏃᎮᏗᏱ ᎤᏂᏩᏛᎲᎩ.
191102.english: For by it the elders obtained a good report.
191102.phonetic: Na-s-gi-ye-no a-nv-di-s-gv e-ti u-na-de-nv-hi o-s-dv ge-tsi-no-he-di-yi u-ni-wa-dv-hv-gi.

191103.cherokee: ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏛᏗᏍᎬ ᎢᏙᎵᎦ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎧᏁᎬ ᎬᏔᏅᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎡᎶᎯ ᏚᏙᏢᏅᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏧᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᎠᎪᏩᏛᏗ ᏥᎩ ᎥᏝ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏔᏅᎯ ᎨᏒ ᏗᎪᏢᏔᏅᎯ ᏱᎩ.
191103.english: Through faith we understand that the worlds were framed by the word of God, so that things which are seen were not made of things which do appear.
191103.phonetic: Go-hi-yu-di ge-sv i-dv-di-s-gv i-do-li-ga U-ne-la-nv-hi ka-ne-gv gv-ta-nv-hi ge-sv e-lo-hi du-do-tlv-nv-i, na-s-gi-no tsu-da-le-nv-dv a-go-wa-dv-di tsi-gi v-tla na-s-gi na gv-ni-ge-sv i-yu-li-s-ta-nv-hi ge-sv di-go-tlv-ta-nv-hi yi-gi.

191104.cherokee: ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎬᏗᏍᎬ ᎡᏈᎵ ᎤᏟ ᎢᏲᏍᏛ ᎤᎵᏍᎪᎸᏓᏁᎴ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎨᎳᏍᏙᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎡᏍᎦᏉ ᎨᏂ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᏛᏁᎸ ᎤᏩᏛᏔᏁ ᎪᎯᏳᏗᏍᎩ ᎤᏓᎤᏗᏳ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏄᏩᏁᎴ ᏚᏓᏂᎸᏤᎸ ᎤᎵᏍᎪᎸᏔᏅᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ; ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏄᏛᏁᎸ ᎬᏗᎭ ᎧᏁᎦ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎤᏲᎱᏒᎯ ᏥᎩ.
191104.english: By faith Abel offered unto God a more excellent sacrifice than Cain, by which he obtained witness that he was righteous, God testifying of his gifts: and by it he being dead yet speaketh.
191104.phonetic: Go-hi-yu-di ge-sv gv-di-s-gv E-qui-li u-tli i-yo-s-dv u-li-s-go-lv-da-ne-le U-ne-la-nv-hi a-tsi-lv--ge-la-s-do-di ge-sv e-s-ga-quo Ge-ni, na-s-gi nu-dv-ne-lv u-wa-dv-ta-ne go-hi-yu-di-s-gi u-da-u-di-yu ge-sv-i, U-ne-la-nv-hi gv-ni-ge-sv nu-wa-ne-le du-da-ni-lv-tse-lv u-li-s-go-lv-ta-nv-hi ge-sv-i; na-s-gi-no nu-dv-ne-lv gv-di-ha ka-ne-ga na-s-quo u-yo-hu-sv-hi tsi-gi.

191105.cherokee: ᎦᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎬᏗᏍᎬ ᎢᎾᎩ ᎠᎦᏘᎾᏫᏛᎮ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏲᎱᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎪᏩᏛᏗᏱ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ; ᎠᎴ ᎥᏝ ᏯᏥᏩᏛᎮᎢ, ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯᏰᏃ ᎤᏘᎾᏫᏛᎮᎢ; ᎠᏏᏉᏰᏃ ᎾᎦᏘᎾᏫᏗᏍᎬᎾ ᎨᏎ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎣᏏᏳ ᎤᏰᎸᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏚᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎲ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᎾᎬᏁᎴᎢ.
191105.english: By faith Enoch was translated that he should not see death; and was not found, because God had translated him: for before his translation he had this testimony, that he pleased God.
191105.phonetic: Ga-hi-yu-di ge-sv gv-di-s-gv I-na-gi a-ga-ti-na-wi-dv-he na-s-gi a-yo-hu-hi-s-di ge-sv u-go-wa-dv-di-yi ni-ge-sv-na; a-le v-tla ya-tsi-wa-dv-he-i, U-ne-la-nv-hi-ye-no u-ti-na-wi-dv-he-i; a-si-quo-ye-no na-ga-ti-na-wi-di-s-gv-na ge-se U-ne-la-nv-hi o-si-yu u-ye-lv-di ge-sv du-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hv gv-ni-ge-sv na-gv-ne-le-i.

191106.cherokee: ᎪᎯᏳᏗᏍᎩᏂ ᎨᏒ ᏂᏛᏗᏍᎬᎾ ᏱᎩ ᎬᏩᏟᏍᏗ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎤᏰᎸᏗ [ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ] ᏗᎩᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗᏱ; ᎩᎶᏰᏃ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏱᎦᎷᏤᎭ ᎠᏎ ᏱᎪᎯᏳᎲᏍᎦ ᎡᎲᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᎫᏴᎡᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎵᏂᎩᏛ ᎬᏩᏲᎯ.
191106.english: But without faith it is impossible to please him: for he that cometh to God must believe that he is, and that he is a rewarder of them that diligently seek him.
191106.phonetic: Go-hi-yu-di-s-gi-ni ge-sv ni-dv-di-s-gv-na yi-gi gv-wa-tli-s-di o-s-dv u-ye-lv-di [U-ne-la-nv-hi] di-gi-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di-yi; gi-lo-ye-no U-ne-la-nv-hi yi-ga-lu-tse-ha a-se yi-go-hi-yu-hv-s-ga e-hv-i, a-le na-s-gi di-gu-yv-e-hi ge-sv u-li-ni-gi-dv gv-wa-yo-hi.

191107.cherokee: ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎬᏗᏍᎬ ᏃᏯ, ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏪᏯᏔᏅ ᎤᏁᎢᏍᏓᏁᎸ ᏧᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᎠᏏ ᎤᎪᎲᎯ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎤᎾᏰᏎᎢ, ᎤᏛᏅᎢᏍᏔᏁ ᏥᏳ ᎤᎾᎵᏍᏕᎸᏙᏗ ᏚᏓᏘᎾᎥᎢ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᏛᏁᎸ ᎤᏮᏔᏁ ᏚᏭᎪᏓᏁᎴ ᎡᎶᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏤᎵ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏚᏳᎪᏛ ᎨᏒ ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏨᏗᏓᎴᎲᏍᎦ.
191107.english: By faith Noah, being warned of God of things not seen as yet, moved with fear, prepared an ark to the saving of his house; by the which he condemned the world, and became heir of the righteousness which is by faith.
191107.phonetic: Go-hi-yu-di ge-sv gv-di-s-gv No-ya, U-ne-la-nv-hi u-we-ya-ta-nv u-ne-i-s-da-ne-lv tsu-da-le-nv-dv a-si u-go-hv-hi ni-ge-sv-na ge-sv-i, u-na-ye-se-i, u-dv-nv-i-s-ta-ne tsi-yu u-na-li-s-de-lv-do-di du-da-ti-na-v-i; na-s-gi nu-dv-ne-lv u-wv-ta-ne du-wu-go-da-ne-le e-lo-hi, a-le u-tse-li nu-li-s-ta-ne na-s-gi du-yu-go-dv ge-sv go-hi-yu-di ge-sv tsv-di-da-le-hv-s-ga.

191108.cherokee: ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎬᏗᏍᎬ ᎡᏆᎭᎻ ᎾᎯᏳ ᎠᏥᏯᏅᎲ ᎢᎸᎯᏢ ᎤᏪᏅᏍᏗᏱ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᎿᎭᎣᏂ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ ᎢᏯᎬᏁᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᏧᏬᎯᏳᏁᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᏧᏄᎪᏤᎢ ᎾᎦᏔᎲ ᎾᎿᎭᎠᎢᏒᎢ.
191108.english: By faith Abraham, when he was called to go out into a place which he should after receive for an inheritance, obeyed; and he went out, not knowing whither he went.
191108.phonetic: Go-hi-yu-di ge-sv gv-di-s-gv E-qua-ha-mi na-hi-yu a-tsi-ya-nv-hv i-lv-hi-tlv u-we-nv-s-di-yi na-s-gi na-hna o-ni i-yv-dv u-tse-li-ga i-ya-gv-ne-di ge-sv-i, tsu-wo-hi-yu-ne-i; a-le tsu-nu-go-tse-i na-ga-ta-hv na-hna a-i-sv-i.

191109.cherokee: ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎬᏗᏍᎬ ᎡᏙᎯᏉ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎤᏪᏙᎴ ᎾᎿᎭᎠᏥᏚᎢᏍᏓᏁᎸ ᎦᏙᎯ, ᏅᏩᎾᏓᎴ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎪᎯ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ, ᏕᎦᎵᏦᏛᏉ ᎠᏁᎮ ᎢᏧᎳᎭ ᎡᏏᎩ ᎠᎴ ᏤᎦᏈ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎾᏠᏯᏍᏗᏍᎩ ᏥᎨᏎ ᎤᏠᏱ ᎠᏚᎢᏍᏔᏅᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ.
191109.english: By faith he sojourned in the land of promise, as in a strange country, dwelling in tabernacles with Isaac and Jacob, the heirs with him of the same promise:
191109.phonetic: Go-hi-yu-di ge-sv gv-di-s-gv e-do-hi-quo i-yu-s-di u-we-do-le na-hna a-tsi-du-i-s-da-ne-lv ga-do-hi, nv-wa-na-da-le u-na-tse-li-go-hi na-s-gi-ya, de-ga-li-tso-dv-quo a-ne-he i-tsu-la-ha E-si-gi a-le Tse-ga-qui, na-s-gi u-na-tlo-ya-s-di-s-gi tsi-ge-se u-tlo-yi a-du-i-s-ta-nv-hi ge-sv-i.

191110.cherokee: ᎠᎦᏖᏃᎮᏰᏃ ᎦᏚᎲᎢ ᏗᎦᎫᏍᏓᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏁᏍᎨᎲᎢ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏬᏢᏅᎯ ᏥᎩ.
191110.english: For he looked for a city which hath foundations, whose builder and maker is God.
191110.phonetic: A-ga-te-no-he-ye-no ga-du-hv-i di-ga-gu-s-da-i, na-s-gi U-ne-la-nv-hi u-ne-s-ge-hv-i a-le u-wo-tlv-nv-hi tsi-gi.

191111.cherokee: ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎬᏗᏍᎬ ᏎᎵ ᎤᏩᏒ ᏧᎵᏂᎪᏎ ᏰᎵᏉ ᎬᏩᏁᎵᎢᏍᏗ ᏥᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏲᎵ ᏧᎾᏄᎪᏫᏎ ᎿᎭᏉ ᏧᏓᎾᏄᎪᏫᏍᏗᏱ ᎤᎶᏐᏁᎸᎯ ᏥᎨᏎᎢ, ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎨ ᏄᏓᎵᏓᏍᏛᎾ ᎤᏰᎸᏅ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎤᏚᎢᏍᏔᏅᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ.
191111.english: Through faith also Sara herself received strength to conceive seed, and was delivered of a child when she was past age, because she judged him faithful who had promised.
191111.phonetic: Go-hi-yu-di ge-sv na-s-quo gv-di-s-gv Se-li u-wa-sv tsu-li-ni-go-se ye-li-quo gv-wa-ne-li-i-s-di tsi-nu-li-s-ta-ne-i, a-le a-yo-li tsu-na-nu-go-wi-se hna-quo tsu-da-na-nu-go-wi-s-di-yi u-lo-so-ne-lv-hi tsi-ge-se-i, nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-ge nu-da-li-da-s-dv-na u-ye-lv-nv na-s-gi na u-du-i-s-ta-nv-hi ge-sv-i.

191112.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎠᏏᏴᏫ ᎨᏒ ᏧᎾᏓᎴᏁᎢ ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏲᎱᏒᎯᏉ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᏃᏈᏏ ᎦᎸᎶ ᏣᏂᎧᎳ ᏥᏄᏂᏧᏈᏍᏗ ᎢᏳᏂᏧᏈᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᏃᏳ ᎠᎺᏉᎯ ᎤᏣᏘ ᏥᎦᎳᎨᏴ ᎬᏎᎰᎲᏍᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᏥᎩ ᎢᏳᏂᏧᏈᏍᏗ.
191112.english: Therefore sprang there even of one, and him as good as dead, so many as the stars of the sky in multitude, and as the sand which is by the sea shore innumerable.
191112.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no a-si-yv-wi ge-sv tsu-na-da-le-ne-i a-le na-s-gi u-yo-hu-sv-hi-quo na-s-gi-ya ge-sv-i, no-qui-si ga-lv-lo tsa-ni-ka-la tsi-nu-ni-tsu-qui-s-di i-yu-ni-tsu-qui-s-di, a-le no-yu a-me-quo-hi u-tsa-ti tsi-ga-la-ge-yv gv-se-ho-hv-s-di ni-ge-sv-na tsi-gi i-yu-ni-tsu-qui-s-di.

191113.cherokee: ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎬᏩᏃᎯᏳᎯ ᏚᏂᏲᎱᏎᎢ, ᏂᏚᎾᏓᏂᎸᏨᎾ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎨᏥᏚᎢᏍᏓᏁᎸᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎢᏅᏍᎩᏂ ᏫᏓᏂᎪᏩᏘᏍᎨᎢ ᎠᎴ ᏄᎾᏜᏓᏏᏛᎡᎲᎾ ᎨᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᏂᏂᏴᎮᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏃᎲᏍᎨ ᏅᏩᎾᏓᎴᏉ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏁᏙᎯᏉ ᎨᏒ ᎠᏂ ᎡᎶᎯ.
191113.english: These all died in faith, not having received the promises, but having seen them afar off, and were persuaded of them, and embraced them, and confessed that they were strangers and pilgrims on the earth.
191113.phonetic: Hi-a na-s-gi ni-ga-dv gv-wa-no-hi-yu-hi du-ni-yo-hu-se-i, ni-du-na-da-ni-lv-tsv-na na-s-gi na ge-tsi-du-i-s-da-ne-lv-hi ge-sv-i, i-nv-s-gi-ni wi-da-ni-go-wa-ti-s-ge-i a-le nu-na-dla-si-dv-e-hv-na ge-se-i, a-le du-ni-ni-yv-he-i, a-le a-ni-no-hv-s-ge nv-wa-na-da-le-quo a-le a-ne-do-hi-quo ge-sv a-ni e-lo-hi.

191114.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏰᏃ ᎯᎠ ᎢᏯᏂᏪᏍᎩ, ᎬᏂᎨᏒᎢᏳ ᎾᏅᏁᎰ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎪᎯ ᎤᏂᏲᎲᎢ.
191114.english: For they that say such things declare plainly that they seek a country.
191114.phonetic: Na-s-gi-ye-no hi-a i-ya-ni-we-s-gi, gv-ni-ge-sv-i-yu na-nv-ne-ho u-na-tse-li-go-hi u-ni-yo-hv-i.

191115.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏰᏃ ᏳᎾᏅᏖ ᎾᎿ ᎭᏧᏂᏄᎪᏨᎢ ᏳᏫᏜᏓᏅᏓᏕᎴᏉ ᎥᎤᎾᏨᏍᏗᏱ;
191115.english: And truly, if they had been mindful of that country from whence they came out, they might have had opportunity to have returned.
191115.phonetic: I-yu-ye-no yu-na-nv-te na-hna tsu-ni-nu-go-tsv-i yu-wi-dla-nv-da-de-le-quo v-u-na-tsv-s-di-yi;

191116.cherokee: ᎪᎯᏍᎩᏂ ᎤᏟ ᎢᏲᏍᏛ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎪᎯ ᎤᏂᏲᎭ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᎸᎶᎢ ᏗᏍᏆᏂᎪᏛᎢ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎥᏝ ᏯᏕᎰᏍᎦ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᎪᏎᏗᏱ; ᏚᏛᏅᎢᏍᏓᏁᎸᏰᏃ ᎦᏚᎲᎢ.
191116.english: But now they desire a better country, that is, an heavenly: wherefore God is not ashamed to be called their God: for he hath prepared for them a city.
191116.phonetic: Go-hi-s-gi-ni u-tli i-yo-s-dv u-na-tse-li-go-hi u-ni-yo-ha, na-s-gi ga-lv-lo-i di-s-qua-ni-go-dv-i; na-s-gi i-yu-s-di U-ne-la-nv-hi v-tla ya-de-ho-s-ga na-s-gi U-ne-la-nv-hi a-go-se-di-yi; du-dv-nv-i-s-da-ne-lv-ye-no ga-du-hv-i.

191117.cherokee: ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎬᏗᏍᎬ ᎡᏆᎭᎻ, ᎾᎯᏳ ᎠᏥᎪᎵᏰᎥ ᎡᏏᎩ ᎤᎵᏍᎪᎸᏔᏁᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎠᏥᏚᎢᏍᏓᏁᎸᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏩᏒᎯᏳ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎤᎵᏍᎪᎸᏔᏁᎢ.
191117.english: By faith Abraham, when he was tried, offered up Isaac: and he that had received the promises offered up his only begotten son,
191117.phonetic: Go-hi-yu-di ge-sv gv-di-s-gv E-qua-ha-mi, na-hi-yu a-tsi-go-li-ye-v E-si-gi u-li-s-go-lv-ta-ne-i; a-le na-s-gi na a-tsi-du-i-s-da-ne-lv-hi ge-sv u-wa-sv-hi-yu u-we-tsi u-li-s-go-lv-ta-ne-i.

191118.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎯᎠ ᎢᏯᏥᏪᏎᎸᎯ ᏥᎨᏎᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎡᏏᎩ ᏚᏙᎥ ᎨᏥᏯᏅᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᏣᏁᏢᏔᏅᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ;
191118.english: Of whom it was said, That in Isaac shall thy seed be called:
191118.phonetic: Na-s-gi na hi-a i-ya-tsi-we-se-lv-hi tsi-ge-se-i, Na-s-gi E-si-gi du-do-v ge-tsi-ya-nv-di-s-ge-s-di tsa-ne-tlv-ta-nv-hi ge-sv-i;

191119.cherokee: ᎤᏁᏔᏅᎯ ᏰᎵᏉ ᏄᎵᏂᎬᎦ ᏧᎴᏙᏗᏱ ᎡᎵᏍᎨᎢ, ᎾᏍᏉ ᎤᏲᎱᏒᎯ ᏱᎩ; ᎾᎿᎭᎾᏍᏉ ᏥᏚᏓᏂᎸᏤ ᏗᏟᎶᏍᏔᏅᎯ ᎾᏍᎩᏯᎢ.
191119.english: Accounting that God was able to raise him up, even from the dead; from whence also he received him in a figure.
191119.phonetic: U-ne-ta-nv-hi ye-li-quo nu-li-ni-gv-ga tsu-le-do-di-yi e-li-s-ge-i, na-s-quo u-yo-hu-sv-hi yi-gi; na-hna na-s-quo tsi-du-da-ni-lv-tse di-tli-lo-s-ta-nv-hi na-s-gi-ya-i.

191120.cherokee: ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎬᏗᏍᎬ ᎡᏏᎩ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏥᏚᏁᏤᎴ ᏤᎦᏈ ᎠᎴ ᎢᏐ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎠᏏ ᎤᎵᏰᎢᎶᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎬᏩᎵ.
191120.english: By faith Isaac blessed Jacob and Esau concerning things to come.
191120.phonetic: Go-hi-yu-di ge-sv gv-di-s-gv E-si-gi o-s-dv tsi-du-ne-tse-le Tse-ga-qui a-le I-so go-hu-s-di a-si u-li-ye-i-lo-hi-s-di ge-sv u-gv-wa-li.

191121.cherokee: ᏤᎦᏈ ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎬᏗᏍᎬᎢ, ᎿᎭᏉ ᎠᏲᎱᏍᎨᎢ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏚᏁᏤᎴ ᎢᏧᎳ ᏦᏩ ᏧᏪᏥ; ᎠᎴ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏔᏁ ᎤᏙᎳᏅᏍᏗᏱ ᎤᏍᎪᎵ [ᎤᎵᏍᏕᎸᏕᎢ.]
191121.english: By faith Jacob, when he was a dying, blessed both the sons of Joseph; and worshipped, leaning upon the top of his staff.
191121.phonetic: Tse-ga-qui go-hi-yu-di ge-sv gv-di-s-gv-i, hna-quo a-yo-hu-s-ge-i o-s-dv du-ne-tse-le i-tsu-la Tso-wa tsu-we-tsi; a-le u-da-do-li-s-ta-ne u-do-la-nv-s-di-yi u-s-go-li [u-li-s-de-lv-de-i.]

191122.cherokee: ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎬᏗᏍᎬ ᏦᏩ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎠᏲᎱᏍᎨᎢ, ᎤᏁᎢᏍᏔᏁ ᎢᏏᎵ ᏧᏪᏥ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏍᏗᏱ; ᎠᎴ ᏧᏁᏤ ᏧᎪᎳ ᎤᎬᏩᎵ.
191122.english: By faith Joseph, when he died, made mention of the departing of the children of Israel; and gave commandment concerning his bones.
191122.phonetic: Go-hi-yu-di ge-sv gv-di-s-gv Tso-wa hna-quo a-yo-hu-s-ge-i, u-ne-i-s-ta-ne I-si-li tsu-we-tsi u-na-da-nv-s-di-yi; a-le tsu-ne-tse tsu-go-la u-gv-wa-li.

191123.cherokee: ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᏅᏗᏍᎬ ᎼᏏ ᏧᎦᏴᎵᎨ ᎾᎯᏳ ᎤᏕᏅ ᏦᎢ ᎢᏯᏅᏙ ᏥᎬᏩᏍᎦᎸᏁᎢ, ᎠᏂᎪᏩᏘᏍᎨᏰᏃ ᎤᏬᏚᎯᏳ ᎨᏒ ᎠᏲᎵ; ᎥᏝ ᎠᎴ ᏯᏂᏍᎦᎢᎮ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎤᏁᏨᎢ.
191123.english: By faith Moses, when he was born, was hid three months of his parents, because they saw he was a proper child; and they were not afraid of the king's commandment.
191123.phonetic: Go-hi-yu-di ge-sv a-nv-di-s-gv Mo-si tsu-ga-yv-li-ge na-hi-yu u-de-nv tso-i i-ya-nv-do tsi-gv-wa-s-ga-lv-ne-i, a-ni-go-wa-ti-s-ge-ye-no u-wo-du-hi-yu ge-sv a-yo-li; v-tla a-le ya-ni-s-ga-i-he u-gv-wi-yu-hi u-ne-tsv-i.

191124.cherokee: ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎬᏗᏍᎬ ᎼᏏ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎤᏔᏅ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅ ᎤᏲᎢᏎᎴᏉ ᏇᎵᏲ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎠᎨᏴ, ᎤᏪᏥ ᎠᎪᏎᏗᏱ;
191124.english: By faith Moses, when he was come to years, refused to be called the son of Pharaoh's daughter;
191124.phonetic: Go-hi-yu-di ge-sv gv-di-s-gv Mo-si hna-quo u-ta-nv nu-li-s-ta-nv u-yo-i-se-le-quo Que-li-yo u-we-tsi a-ge-yv, u-we-tsi a-go-se-di-yi;

191125.cherokee: ᎤᏑᏰᎭ ᎤᏟ ᎤᏰᎸᏁ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏧᏤᎵ ᏴᏫ ᎠᏂᎩᎵᏲᏥᏙᎲ ᎤᏠᏯᏍᏙᏗᏱ, ᎠᏃ ᎠᏍᎦᏂ ᏞᎦ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎤᏓᏓᏅᏓᏗᏍᏙᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ;
191125.english: Choosing rather to suffer affliction with the people of God, than to enjoy the pleasures of sin for a season;
191125.phonetic: U-su-ye-ha u-tli u-ye-lv-ne U-ne-la-nv-hi tsu-tse-li yv-wi a-ni-gi-li-yo-tsi-do-hv u-tlo-ya-s-do-di-yi, a-no a-s-ga-ni tle-ga o-s-dv u-da-da-nv-da-di-s-do-di ge-sv-i;

191126.cherokee: ᎤᏟ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎡᎵᏍᎨ ᎦᏰᏥᏐᏢᏙᏗᏱ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ, ᎠᏃ ᏧᎬᏩᎶᏗ ᎠᏍᏆᏂᎪᏛ ᎢᏥᏈᏱ; ᎠᎦᎫᏴᎡᏗᏰᏃ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏫᏓᎧᏂᏍᎨᎢ.
191126.english: Esteeming the reproach of Christ greater riches than the treasures in Egypt: for he had respect unto the recompence of the reward.
191126.phonetic: U-tli ga-lv-quo-di-yu e-li-s-ge ga-ye-tsi-so-tlv-do-di-yi Ga-lo-ne-dv ge-sv i-yu-s-di, a-no tsu-gv-wa-lo-di a-s-qua-ni-go-dv I-tsi-qui-yi; a-ga-gu-yv-e-di-ye-no ge-sv i-yv-dv wi-da-ka-ni-s-ge-i.

191127.cherokee: ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎬᏗᏍᎬ ᎢᏥᏈᏱ ᎤᏓᏅᏎᎢ, ᎾᏍᎦᎢᎲᎾ ᎨᏎ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎤᏔᎳᏬᏍᎬᎢ; ᎬᏂᏗᏳᏍᎩᏂ ᎨᏒ ᎾᏍᎩᏯᏉ ᏣᎪᏩᏘᏍᎪ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎦᏰᏥᎪᏩᏛᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ.
191127.english: By faith he forsook Egypt, not fearing the wrath of the king: for he endured, as seeing him who is invisible.
191127.phonetic: Go-hi-yu-di ge-sv gv-di-s-gv I-tsi-qui-yi u-da-nv-se-i, na-s-ga-i-hv-na ge-se u-gv-wi-yu-hi u-ta-la-wo-s-gv-i; gv-ni-di-yu-s-gi-ni ge-sv na-s-gi-ya-quo tsa-go-wa-ti-s-go na-s-gi na ga-ye-tsi-go-wa-dv-di ni-ge-sv-na ge-sv-i.

191128.cherokee: ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎬᏗᏍᎬ ᎤᏁᏤ ᎧᏃᎯᏰᎩ ᏗᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗᏱ ᎤᏂᏍᏆᏂᎪᏙᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᎩᎬ ᎤᏂᏍᏚᏟᏍᏗᏱ [ᎦᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ,] ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᎬᏱ ᎤᎾᏕᏅᎯ ᏗᏛᏗᏍᎩ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎤᏅᏁᏗᏱ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ.
191128.english: Through faith he kept the passover, and the sprinkling of blood, lest he that destroyed the firstborn should touch them.
191128.phonetic: Go-hi-yu-di ge-sv gv-di-s-gv u-ne-tse ka-no-hi-ye-gi di-li-s-da-yv-di-yi u-ni-s-qua-ni-go-do-di-yi, a-le gi-gv u-ni-s-du-tli-s-di-yi [ga-lo-hi-s-di-yi,] na-s-gi i-gv-yi u-na-de-nv-hi di-dv-di-s-gi go-hu-s-di u-nv-ne-di-yi ni-ge-sv-na.

191129.cherokee: ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᏅᏗᏍᎬ ᎠᎺᏉᎯᎩᎦᎨᏍᏛᏱ ᎠᏰᎵ ᎤᏂᎶᏎ ᎤᎧᏲᏛᎯ ᏣᏂᎶᏍᎪ ᎾᏍᎩᏯᎢ; ᎢᏥᏈᏱᏃ ᎠᏁᎯ ᎤᎾᏁᎶᏔᏅ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᎾᏛᏁᏗᏱ ᏚᏂᎬᏤᎢ.
191129.english: By faith they passed through the Red sea as by dry land: which the Egyptians assaying to do were drowned.
191129.phonetic: Go-hi-yu-di ge-sv a-nv-di-s-gv a-me-quo-hi-gi-ga-ge-s-dv-yi a-ye-li u-ni-lo-se u-ka-yo-dv-hi tsa-ni-lo-s-go na-s-gi-ya-i; I-tsi-qui-yi-no a-ne-hi u-na-ne-lo-ta-nv na-s-gi i-yu-na-dv-ne-di-yi du-ni-gv-tse-i.

191130.cherokee: ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᏅᏗᏍᎬ ᏓᏐᏴ ᏤᎵᎪ ᏚᏪᏒᏙᎠᏎᎢ, ᎿᎭᏉ ᎦᎵᏉᎩ ᏧᏒᎯᏛ ᏂᏚᎾᏚᏫᏍᏔᏁᎢ.
191130.english: By faith the walls of Jericho fell down, after they were compassed about seven days.
191130.phonetic: Go-hi-yu-di ge-sv a-nv-di-s-gv da-so-yv Tse-li-go du-we-sv-do-a-se-i, hna-quo ga-li-quo-gi tsu-sv-hi-dv ni-du-na-du-wi-s-ta-ne-i.

191131.cherokee: ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎬᏗᏍᎬ ᎴᎭᏫ ᎤᏁᎫᏥᏛ ᎥᏝ ᏯᏥᏛᏔᏁ ᎨᏥᏛᏔᏅ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᏄᏃᎯᏳᏒᎾ ᏥᎨᏎᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏙᎯᏱ ᏥᏚᏓᏂᎸᏤ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎤᏂᎦᏛᏂᏙᎯ.
191131.english: By faith the harlot Rahab perished not with them that believed not, when she had received the spies with peace.
191131.phonetic: Go-hi-yu-di ge-sv gv-di-s-gv Le-ha-wi u-ne-gu-tsi-dv v-tla ya-tsi-dv-ta-ne ge-tsi-dv-ta-nv na-s-gi na nu-no-hi-yu-sv-na tsi-ge-se-i, na-s-gi do-hi-yi tsi-du-da-ni-lv-tse i-yu-s-di u-ni-ga-dv-ni-do-hi.

191132.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎦᏙ ᎠᏏ ᏓᎦᏛᏂ?  ᎥᏝᏰᏃ ᎬᎩᏍᏆᏗᏍᏗ ᏱᎩ ᏱᎦᏥᏃᎮᎸ ᎩᏗᏯᏂ, ᎠᎴ ᏇᎴᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏌᎻᏏᏂ, ᎠᎴ ᏤᏈᏓ; ᏕᏫ ᎾᏍᏉ, ᎠᎴ ᏌᎻ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎾᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ.
191132.english: And what shall I more say? for the time would fail me to tell of Gedeon, and of Barak, and of Samson, and of Jephthae; of David also, and Samuel, and of the prophets:
191132.phonetic: A-le ga-do a-si da-ga-dv-ni?  v-tla-ye-no gv-gi-s-qua-di-s-di yi-gi yi-ga-tsi-no-he-lv Gi-di-ya-ni, a-le Que-le-gi, a-le Sa-mi-si-ni, a-le Tse-qui-da; De-wi na-s-quo, a-le Sa-mi, a-le a-na-do-le-ho-s-gi.

191133.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᏅᏗᏍᎬ ᎠᏰᎵ ᏕᎪᏢᏒ ᏥᏓᎾᏓᎵᏁᎯᏕᎮᎢ, ᏚᏳᎪᏛ ᏥᏚᏂᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎮᎢ, ᎤᎾᏤᎵ ᏥᎾᏅᏁᎮ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎨᏥᏚᎢᏍᏓᎸᎢ, ᏢᏓᏥ ᎤᏂᏃᏕᏅ ᏗᏂᎰᎵ ᏥᏓᏂᏍᏚᎲᏍᎨᎢ,
191133.english: Who through faith subdued kingdoms, wrought righteousness, obtained promises, stopped the mouths of lions,
191133.phonetic: Na-s-gi go-hi-yu-di ge-sv a-nv-di-s-gv a-ye-li de-go-tlv-sv tsi-da-na-da-li-ne-hi-de-he-i, du-yu-go-dv tsi-du-ni-lv-wi-s-da-ne-he-i, u-na-tse-li tsi-na-nv-ne-he nu-s-dv ge-tsi-du-i-s-da-lv-i, tlv-da-tsi u-ni-no-de-nv di-ni-ho-li tsi-da-ni-s-du-hv-s-ge-i,

191134.cherokee: ᎠᏥᎸ ᎤᏍᎦᏎᏗ ᏣᏅᏜᏓᏗᏍᎨᎢ, ᎠᏰᎳᏍᏗ-ᎦᏅᎯᏛ ᏣᎾᏗᏫᏎᎮᎢ, ᏗᏂᏩᎾᎦᎳ ᎨᏒ ᏧᎾᎵᏂᎩᏛ ᏥᏂᎨᎬᏁᎮᎢ, ᎾᏂᏍᎦᎢᎲᎾ ᏂᎾᎾᎵᏍᏗᏍᎨ ᏓᎾᎵᎲᎢ, ᏥᏓᏂᎨᎯᏙᎮ ᏅᏩᎾᏓᎴ ᎠᏂᏯᏫᏍᎩ.
191134.english: Quenched the violence of fire, escaped the edge of the sword, out of weakness were made strong, waxed valiant in fight, turned to flight the armies of the aliens.
191134.phonetic: A-tsi-lv u-s-ga-se-di tsa-nv-dla-di-s-ge-i, a-ye-la-s-di--ga-nv-hi-dv tsa-na-di-wi-se-he-i, di-ni-wa-na-ga-la ge-sv tsu-na-li-ni-gi-dv tsi-ni-ge-gv-ne-he-i, na-ni-s-ga-i-hv-na ni-na-na-li-s-di-s-ge da-na-li-hv-i, tsi-da-ni-ge-hi-do-he nv-wa-na-da-le a-ni-ya-wi-s-gi.

191135.cherokee: ᎠᏂᎨᏴ ᏗᎬᏩᏂᏲᎱᎯᏎᎸᎯ ᏗᏅᏃᏛ ᏔᎵᏁ ᏂᏙᎨᏥᏲᎯᏎᎮᎢ; ᎢᎦᏛᏃ ᏥᎨᏥᎩᎸᏍᎨᎢ, ᏂᏓᎾᏓᏂᎸᎬᎾ ᎨᎫᏓᎴᏍᎬᎢ; ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎨ ᎤᏟ ᎢᏲᏍᏛ ᎤᏂᏩᏛᏗᏱ ᎤᎾᏚᎵᏍᎬ ᏗᎴᎯᏐᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ;
191135.english: Women received their dead raised to life again: and others were tortured, not accepting deliverance; that they might obtain a better resurrection:
191135.phonetic: A-ni-ge-yv di-gv-wa-ni-yo-hu-hi-se-lv-hi di-nv-no-dv ta-li-ne ni-do-ge-tsi-yo-hi-se-he-i; i-ga-dv-no tsi-ge-tsi-gi-lv-s-ge-i, ni-da-na-da-ni-lv-gv-na ge-gu-da-le-s-gv-i; nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-ge u-tli i-yo-s-dv u-ni-wa-dv-di-yi u-na-du-li-s-gv di-le-hi-so-di ge-sv-i;

191136.cherokee: ᎢᎦᏛᏃ ᎨᏥᎪᎵᏰᏍᎨ ᎦᎨᏥᏐᏢᏗᏍᎬ ᎠᎴ ᏕᎨᏥᎵᎥᏂᎲᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏕᎨᎦᎸᎢᎲ ᎠᎴ ᏕᎨᏥᏍᏚᎲᏍᎬᎢ;
191136.english: And others had trial of cruel mockings and scourgings, yea, moreover of bonds and imprisonment:
191136.phonetic: I-ga-dv-no ge-tsi-go-li-ye-s-ge ga-ge-tsi-so-tlv-di-s-gv a-le de-ge-tsi-li-v-ni-hv-i, a-le na-s-quo de-ge-ga-lv-i-hv a-le de-ge-tsi-s-du-hv-s-gv-i;

191137.cherokee: ᏅᏯ ᏕᎨᎬᏂᏍᏗᏍᎨᎢ, ᏗᎦᏄᏙᎩ ᎬᏗ ᏕᎨᏥᎦᎵᏍᎨᎢ, ᏕᎨᏥᎪᎵᏰᏍᎨᎢ, ᎠᏰᎳᏍᏗ-ᎦᏅᎯᏛ ᏕᎨᏥᎢᏍᏗᏍᎨᎢ; ᎠᏫ ᎤᏂᏃᏕᎾ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏫ ᏗᏂᎭᏄᎸᎯ ᏧᏂᏁᎦᎸᏅᎯ ᏧᎾᏄᏩᎢ ᎠᏁᏙᎮᎢ; ᎤᏂᏂᎬᏎᎯ ᎨᏎᎢ, ᎠᏂᎩᎵᏲᏥᏙᎮᎢ.
191137.english: They were stoned, they were sawn asunder, were tempted, were slain with the sword: they wandered about in sheepskins and goatskins; being destitute, afflicted, tormented;
191137.phonetic: Nv-ya de-ge-gv-ni-s-di-s-ge-i, di-ga-nu-do-gi gv-di de-ge-tsi-ga-li-s-ge-i, de-ge-tsi-go-li-ye-s-ge-i, a-ye-la-s-di--ga-nv-hi-dv de-ge-tsi-i-s-di-s-ge-i; a-wi u-ni-no-de-na a-le a-wi di-ni-ha-nu-lv-hi tsu-ni-ne-ga-lv-nv-hi tsu-na-nu-wa-i a-ne-do-he-i; u-ni-ni-gv-se-hi ge-se-i, a-ni-gi-li-yo-tsi-do-he-i.

191138.cherokee: [ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎥᏝ ᎡᎶᎯ ᎬᏩᎾᏕᏗ ᏱᎨᏎᎢ;] ᎢᎾᎨ ᎠᏁᏙᎮᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏙᏓᎸᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᏍᏓᎦᎸᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎦᏙᎯ ᏓᏔᎴᏒᎢ.
191138.english: (Of whom the world was not worthy:) they wandered in deserts, and in mountains, and in dens and caves of the earth.
191138.phonetic: [Na-s-gi hi-a v-tla e-lo-hi gv-wa-na-de-di yi-ge-se-i;] i-na-ge a-ne-do-he-i, a-le do-da-lv-i, a-le du-s-da-ga-lv-i, a-le ga-do-hi da-ta-le-sv-i.

191139.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎯᎠ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎨᏥᏃᎮᏍᎩ ᏥᎨᏎ ᎤᏃᎯᏳᏒ ᎢᏳᏩᏂᏌᏛ, ᎥᏝ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎨᏥᏚᎢᏍᏓᏁᎸ ᏱᏚᎾᏓᏂᎸᏤᎢ;
191139.english: And these all, having obtained a good report through faith, received not the promise:
191139.phonetic: A-le na-s-gi ni-ga-dv hi-a o-s-dv ge-tsi-no-he-s-gi tsi-ge-se u-no-hi-yu-sv i-yu-wa-ni-sa-dv, v-tla nu-s-dv ge-tsi-du-i-s-da-ne-lv yi-du-na-da-ni-lv-tse-i;

191140.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎢᎦᏛᏅᎢᏍᏓᏁᎸᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎤᏟ ᎢᏲᏍᏛ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᏂᎷᎶᏤᎲᎾ ᎢᎨᎬᏁᏗᏱ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎠᏴ ᏂᎦᏠᏯᏍᏗᏍᎬᎾ.
191140.english: God having provided some better thing for us, that they without us should not be made perfect.
191140.phonetic: Na-s-gi U-ne-la-nv-hi i-ga-dv-nv-i-s-da-ne-lv-hi ge-sv i-yu-s-di go-hu-s-di u-tli i-yo-s-dv, na-s-gi nu-ni-lu-lo-tse-hv-na i-ge-gv-ne-di-yi ni-ge-sv-na a-yv ni-ga-tlo-ya-s-di-s-gv-na.

191201.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎢᏳᏂᏧᏈᏍᏗ ᎠᏂᎦᏔᎯ ᏥᏕᎨᎦᏚᏫᏍᏗ, ᎢᏴᏛ ᏂᏛᏁᏓ ᏄᏓᎴᏒ ᎦᎨᏛ ᎢᎬᏁᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏍᎦᏂ ᎠᎯᏛ ᎨᎦᏢᏔᏍᏗ ᏥᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᏛᏂᏗᏳ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎢᏓᏙᎩᏯᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎠᏙᎩᏯᏍᏗ ᎢᎬᏱᏗᏢ ᏁᎬᏁᎸᎢ,
191201.english: Wherefore seeing we also are compassed about with so great a cloud of witnesses, let us lay aside every weight, and the sin which doth so easily beset us, and let us run with patience the race that is set before us,
191201.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no i-yu-ni-tsu-qui-s-di a-ni-ga-ta-hi tsi-de-ge-ga-du-wi-s-di, i-yv-dv ni-dv-ne-da nu-da-le-sv ga-ge-dv i-gv-ne-hi, a-le a-s-ga-ni a-hi-dv ge-ga-tlv-ta-s-di tsi-gi, a-le di-dv-ni-di-yu ge-se-s-di i-da-do-gi-ya-s-ge-s-di a-do-gi-ya-s-di i-gv-yi-di-tlv ne-gv-ne-lv-i,

191202.cherokee: ᏫᏕᏓᎦᏂᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᏥᏌ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏩᏂᏌᏛ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏍᏆᏗᏍᎩ ᏥᎩ ᎢᎪᎯᏳᏒᎢ.  ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎵᎮᎵᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎢᎬᏱᏗᏢ ᎾᎬᏁᎸ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎬᏂᏗᏳ ᏓᏓᎿᎭᏩᏍᏛ ᎤᎩᎵᏲᏤᎢ, ᎤᎵᏌᎵᏉ ᎤᏰᎸᏎ ᎤᏕᎰᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎦᏘᏏᏗᏢ ᎤᏪᏅ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏪᏍᎩᎸᎢ.
191202.english: Looking unto Jesus the author and finisher of our faith; who for the joy that was set before him endured the cross, despising the shame, and is set down at the right hand of the throne of God.
191202.phonetic: Wi-de-da-ga-ni-s-ge-s-di Tsi-sa na-s-gi i-yu-wa-ni-sa-dv, a-le a-s-qua-di-s-gi tsi-gi i-go-hi-yu-sv-i.  Na-s-gi u-li-he-li-s-di ge-sv i-gv-yi-di-tlv na-gv-ne-lv i-yu-s-di gv-ni-di-yu da-da-hna-wa-s-dv u-gi-li-yo-tse-i, u-li-sa-li-quo u-ye-lv-se u-de-ho-hi-s-di ge-sv-i, a-le a-ga-ti-si-di-tlv u-we-nv U-ne-la-nv-hi u-we-s-gi-lv-i.

191203.cherokee: ᎡᏣᏓᏅᏛᎵᏉᏍᎩᏂ Ꮎ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᎦᎢ ᎦᎬᏩᏐᏢᏛ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᎾ ᎤᏁᎳᎩ ᏚᏪᎵᏎᎸᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏞᏍᏗ ᏗᏥᏯᏪᏨᎩ ᎠᎴ ᏞᏍᏗ ᏧᏩᎾᎦᎶᏨᎩ ᏕᏣᏓᏅᏛᎢ.
191203.english: For consider him that endured such contradiction of sinners against himself, lest ye be wearied and faint in your minds.
191203.phonetic: E-tsa-da-nv-dv-li-quo-s-gi-ni na na-s-gi i-ga-i ga-gv-wa-so-tlv-dv a-ni-s-ga-na u-ne-la-gi du-we-li-se-lv-i, na-s-gi-no tle-s-di di-tsi-ya-we-tsv-gi a-le tle-s-di tsu-wa-na-ga-lo-tsv-gi de-tsa-da-nv-dv-i.

191204.cherokee: ᎥᏝ ᎠᏏ ᎢᏥᎩᎬ ᎤᏨᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏱᏗᏣᏟᏴᎲ ᎠᏍᎦᏂ ᏕᏣᏟᏴᎡᎲᎢ.
191204.english: Ye have not yet resisted unto blood, striving against sin.
191204.phonetic: V-tla a-si i-tsi-gi-gv u-tsv-i-s-di-yi i-yv-dv yi-di-tsa-tli-yv-hv a-s-ga-ni de-tsa-tli-yv-e-hv-i.

191205.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎢᏨᎨᏫᏒᎯ ᎢᎩ ᎡᏥᏬᏁᏗᏍᎬ ᏗᏂᏲᎵ ᏥᎨᏥᏬᏁᏗᏍᎪ ᎾᏍᎩᏯᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏥᏂᎦᏪᎭ, ᎠᏇᏥ ᏞᏍᏗ ᏅᎵᏌᎵ ᏱᏣᏰᎸᏎᏍᏗ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏣᎩᎵᏲᎢᏍᏗᏍᎬᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏞᏍᏗ ᏩᎾ ᎯᏳᏏᎦᎶᎨᏍᏗ ᏣᎬᏍᎪᎸᎢᏍᎨᏍᏗ,
191205.english: And ye have forgotten the exhortation which speaketh unto you as unto children, My son, despise not thou the chastening of the Lord, nor faint when thou art rebuked of him:
191205.phonetic: A-le i-tsv-ge-wi-sv-hi i-gi e-tsi-wo-ne-di-s-gv di-ni-yo-li tsi-ge-tsi-wo-ne-di-s-go na-s-gi-ya-i, hi-a tsi-ni-ga-we-ha, A-que-tsi tle-s-di nv-li-sa-li yi-tsa-ye-lv-se-s-di U-gv-wi-yu-hi tsa-gi-li-yo-i-s-di-s-gv-i, a-le tle-s-di wa-na hi-yu-si-ga-lo-ge-s-di tsa-gv-s-go-lv-i-s-ge-s-di,

191206.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏰᏃ Ꮎ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎤᎨᏳᎯ ᎠᎩᎵᏲᎢᏍᏗᏍᎪᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏕᎦᎵᎥᏂᎰ ᎾᏂᎥ ᏧᏪᏥ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏧᏓᏂᎸᏨᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ.
191206.english: For whom the Lord loveth he chasteneth, and scourgeth every son whom he receiveth.
191206.phonetic: Na-s-gi-ye-no na U-gv-wi-yu-hi u-ge-yu-hi a-gi-li-yo-i-s-di-s-go-i, a-le de-ga-li-v-ni-ho na-ni-v tsu-we-tsi na-s-gi tsu-da-ni-lv-tsv-hi ge-sv-i.

191207.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏃ ᎡᏥᎩᎵᏲᎢᏍᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᏧᏪᏥ ᏂᏓᏛᏁᎲ ᏂᏣᏛᏁᎭ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ; ᎦᎪᏰᏃ ᎡᎭ ᎠᏲᎵ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏙᏓ ᏄᎩᎵᏲᎢᏍᏗᏍᎬᎾ?
191207.english: If ye endure chastening, God dealeth with you as with sons; for what son is he whom the father chasteneth not?
191207.phonetic: I-yu-no e-tsi-gi-li-yo-i-s-di-s-ge-s-di, na-s-gi-ya tsu-we-tsi ni-da-dv-ne-hv ni-tsa-dv-ne-ha U-ne-la-nv-hi; ga-go-ye-no e-ha a-yo-li na-s-gi u-do-da nu-gi-li-yo-i-s-di-s-gv-na?

191208.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏍᎩᏂ ᎢᎸᎯᏳ ᏁᏥᎩᎵᏲᎢᏍᏗᏍᎬᎾ ᏱᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏂᎦᏗᏳ ᏗᏂᏲᎵ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᎨᎬᏁᏗ ᏥᎩ, ᎿᎭᏉ ᎢᏴᏛᏉ ᎢᏣᏕᏂᏙᎸᎯ, ᎥᏝᏃ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎢᏣᏕᏅᎯ ᏱᎩ.
191208.english: But if ye be without chastisement, whereof all are partakers, then are ye bastards, and not sons.
191208.phonetic: I-yu-s-gi-ni i-lv-hi-yu ne-tsi-gi-li-yo-i-s-di-s-gv-na yi-gi, na-s-gi ni-ga-di-yu di-ni-yo-li na-s-gi i-ge-gv-ne-di tsi-gi, hna-quo i-yv-dv-quo i-tsa-de-ni-do-lv-hi, v-tla-no o-s-dv i-tsa-de-nv-hi yi-gi.

191209.cherokee: ᎠᎴᏬ, ᎤᏇᏓᎵ ᎨᏒ ᏗᎩᏙᏓ ᏕᎨᎲᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᎨᎩᎩᎵᏲᎢᏍᏗᏍᎬᎩ; ᎠᎴ ᎨᏗᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᏥᎨᏒᎩ; ᏝᏍᎪ ᎤᏟ ᏱᏂᏙᎬᏩᏳᎪᏗ ᏤᏓᏓᏲᎯᏎᏗᏱ ᏗᎦᏓᏅᏙ ᏧᏬᏢᏅᎯ ᏥᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᎦᏛᏂᏗᏍᏗᏱ?
191209.english: Furthermore we have had fathers of our flesh which corrected us, and we gave them reverence: shall we not much rather be in subjection unto the Father of spirits, and live?
191209.phonetic: A-le-wo, u-que-da-li ge-sv Di-gi-do-da de-ge-hv-gi, na-s-gi tsi-ge-gi-gi-li-yo-i-s-di-s-gv-gi; a-le ge-di-lv-quo-di-yu tsi-ge-sv-gi; tla-s-go u-tli yi-ni-do-gv-wa-yu-go-di tse-da-da-yo-hi-se-di-yi di-ga-da-nv-do tsu-wo-tlv-nv-hi tsi-gi, a-le i-ga-dv-ni-di-s-di-yi?

191210.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏰᏃ Ꮎ ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯ ᏞᎦ ᎨᎩᎩᎵᏲᎢᏍᏗᏍᎬᎩ ᎤᏅᏒ ᎠᎾᏓᏅᏖᏍᎬ ᎾᎾᏛᏁᎲᎩ; [ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯᏍᎩᏂ] ᎣᏍᏛ ᎢᎦᎵᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎤᎬᏩᎸ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏣᏠᏯᏍᏗᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎠᏍᎦᎾ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ.
191210.english: For they verily for a few days chastened us after their own pleasure; but he for our profit, that we might be partakers of his holiness.
191210.phonetic: Na-s-gi-ye-no na u-do-hi-yu-hi tle-ga ge-gi-gi-li-yo-i-s-di-s-gv-gi u-nv-sv a-na-da-nv-te-s-gv na-na-dv-ne-hv-gi; [u-ne-la-nv-hi-s-gi-ni] o-s-dv i-ga-li-s-da-ne-di u-gv-wa-lv, na-s-gi i-tsa-tlo-ya-s-di-s-gi i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi u-wa-sv a-s-ga-na ni-ge-sv-na ge-sv-i.

191211.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᏂᎦᎥᏉ ᎠᎩᎵᏲᎢᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎪᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎥᏝ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎣᏓᏅᏓᏗᏍᏗᏍᎩ ᏱᎩ, ᎤᏲᏉᏍᎩᏂ; ᎣᏂᏍᎩᏂᏃᏅ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏅᏩᏙᎯᏯᏛ ᎠᏓᏅᏓᏗᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎦᎾᏄᎪᏫᏍᎪ ᏚᏳᎪᏛ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎾᏄᎪᏫᏒᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᎾᎵᏍᏓᏁᎸᎯ.
191211.english: Now no chastening for the present seemeth to be joyous, but grievous: nevertheless afterward it yieldeth the peaceable fruit of righteousness unto them which are exercised thereby.
191211.phonetic: A-le ni-ga-v-quo a-gi-li-yo-i-s-di ge-sv go-hi ge-sv v-tla o-s-dv o-da-nv-da-di-s-di-s-gi yi-gi, u-yo-quo-s-gi-ni; o-ni-s-gi-ni-no-nv i-yv-dv nv-wa-do-hi-ya-dv a-da-nv-da-di-s-di ge-sv ga-na-nu-go-wi-s-go du-yu-go-dv ge-sv u-na-nu-go-wi-sv-hi, na-s-gi i-yu-na-li-s-da-ne-lv-hi.

191212.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏗᏥᏌᎳᏛᎦ ᏗᏦᏰᏂ ᎡᎳᏗ ᎢᏧᎵᏍᏔᏅᎯ ᏥᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᏥᏂᎨᏂ ᏗᏩᎾᎦᎳᎢ;
191212.english: Wherefore lift up the hands which hang down, and the feeble knees;
191212.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di di-tsi-sa-la-dv-ga di-tso-ye-ni e-la-di i-tsu-li-s-ta-nv-hi tsi-gi, a-le di-tsi-ni-ge-ni di-wa-na-ga-la-i;

191213.cherokee: ᎥᎾᏕᏍᎩ ᏂᏨᎦ ᏕᎦᏅᏅ ᎢᏥᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎠᏲᎤᎵ ᎨᏒ ᏞᏍᏗ ᏳᏂᎪᎸᏍᏔᏁᏍᏗ; ᎧᏅᏬᏗᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
191213.english: And make straight paths for your feet, lest that which is lame be turned out of the way; but let it rather be healed.
191213.phonetic: V-na-de-s-gi ni-tsv-ga de-ga-nv-nv i-tsi-lo-hi-s-di-yi, na-s-gi-no a-yo-u-li ge-sv tle-s-di yu-ni-go-lv-s-ta-ne-s-di; ka-nv-wo-di-quo-s-gi-ni ge-se-s-di.

191214.cherokee: ᏅᏩᏙᎯᏯᏛ ᏕᏥᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏎᏍᏗ ᎾᏂᎥᏉ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏕᏣᏓᏛᏗᏍᎬᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎦᏅᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎧᏂᎩᏛ ᏱᎩ ᎾᏍᎩ, ᎥᏝ ᎩᎶ ᏰᎵ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎬᏩᎪᏩᏛᏗ ᏱᎩ;
191214.english: Follow peace with all men, and holiness, without which no man shall see the Lord:
191214.phonetic: Nv-wa-do-hi-ya-dv de-tsi-ka-hna-wa-di-se-s-di na-ni-v-quo go-hu-s-di de-tsa-da-dv-di-s-gv-i, a-le na-s-ga-nv-na ge-sv-i, ka-ni-gi-dv yi-gi na-s-gi, v-tla gi-lo ye-li U-gv-wi-yu-hi gv-wa-go-wa-dv-di yi-gi;

191215.cherokee: ᎢᏤᏯᏔᎯᏳ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏞᏍᏗ ᎩᎶ ᎡᏍᎦ ᎤᏬᎭᏒᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎬᏩᎦᏘᏯ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ; ᏞᏍᏗ ᎤᏴᏍᏗ ᎤᎿᎭᏍᏕᏢ ᎦᎾᏄᎪᎬ ᎢᏣᏕᏯᏙᏔᏅᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏩᏂᏌᏛ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᎦᏓᎭ ᎢᏳᎾᎵᏍᏔᏅᎩ;
191215.english: Looking diligently lest any man fail of the grace of God; lest any root of bitterness springing up trouble you, and thereby many be defiled;
191215.phonetic: I-tse-ya-ta-hi-yu ge-se-s-di na-s-gi-no tle-s-di gi-lo e-s-ga u-wo-ha-sv-gi U-ne-la-nv-hi gv-wa-ga-ti-ya u-da-do-li-s-di ge-sv-i; tle-s-di u-yv-s-di u-hna-s-de-tlv ga-na-nu-go-gv i-tsa-de-ya-do-ta-nv-gi, a-le na-s-gi i-yu-wa-ni-sa-dv u-ni-tsa-ti ga-da-ha i-yu-na-li-s-ta-nv-gi;

191216.cherokee: ᏞᏍᏗ ᎩᎶ ᎤᏕᎵᏛ ᏗᏂᏏᎲᏍᎩ ᏱᏣᏓᏑᏰᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏍᎦᎾ ᏴᏫ, ᎢᏐ ᎾᏍᎩᏯᎢ, ᎤᏍᎩ ᏌᏉᏉ ᎢᏯᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗ ᏥᏚᎬᏩᎶᏔᏁ ᏧᎾᏗᏅᏎ ᎢᎬᏱ ᎡᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ.
191216.english: Lest there be any fornicator, or profane person, as Esau, who for one morsel of meat sold his birthright.
191216.phonetic: Tle-s-di gi-lo u-de-li-dv di-ni-si-hv-s-gi yi-tsa-da-su-ye-s-di, a-le a-s-ga-na yv-wi, I-so na-s-gi-ya-i, u-s-gi sa-quo-quo i-ya-li-s-da-yv-di tsi-du-gv-wa-lo-ta-ne tsu-na-di-nv-se i-gv-yi e-hi ge-sv-i.

191217.cherokee: ᎢᏥᎦᏔᎭᏰᏃ ᎣᏂ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎢᏯᎦᏛᏁᏗᏱ ᏧᏚᎵᏍᎨ ᏣᏥᏐᏅᎢᏍᏔᏁᎢ; ᎥᏝᏰᏃ [ᎤᏙᏓ] ᎤᏓᏅᏛ ᎬᏩᏁᏟᏴᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏳᏩᏛᎮᎢ, ᎤᎵᏏᎾᎯᏍᏗᏳᏍᎩᏂᏃᏅ ᎠᎴ ᏗᎬᎦᏌᏬᎢᎯ ᎤᏲᎴᎢ.
191217.english: For ye know how that afterward, when he would have inherited the blessing, he was rejected: for he found no place of repentance, though he sought it carefully with tears.
191217.phonetic: I-tsi-ga-ta-ha-ye-no o-ni i-yv-dv o-s-dv i-ya-ga-dv-ne-di-yi tsu-du-li-s-ge tsa-tsi-so-nv-i-s-ta-ne-i; v-tla-ye-no [u-do-da] u-da-nv-dv gv-wa-ne-tli-yv-s-di ge-sv yu-wa-dv-he-i, u-li-si-na-hi-s-di-yu-s-gi-ni-no-nv a-le di-gv-ga-sa-wo-i-hi u-yo-le-i.

191218.cherokee: ᎥᏝᏰᏃ ᎣᏓᎸ ᎬᏒᏂᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏱᏥᎷᏨ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏣᏓᏪᎵᎩᏍᎨᎢ, ᎥᏝ ᎠᎴ ᎬᎿᎭᎨ ᎤᎶᎩᎸᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎵᏏᎩᏳ ᎰᏒ ᎠᎴ ᎦᏃᎸᎥᏍᎬᎢ,
191218.english: For ye are not come unto the mount that might be touched, and that burned with fire, nor unto blackness, and darkness, and tempest,
191218.phonetic: V-tla-ye-no o-da-lv gv-sv-ni-s-di ge-sv yi-tsi-lu-tsv, a-le na-s-gi tsa-da-we-li-gi-s-ge-i, v-tla a-le gv-hna-ge u-lo-gi-lv-i, a-le u-li-si-gi-yu ho-sv a-le ga-no-lv-v-s-gv-i,

191219.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎠᏤᎷᎩ ᎤᏃᏴᎬᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎧᏁᎬ ᎤᏃᏴᎬᎢ; ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏅᎯ ᎤᏂᏔᏲᎴ ᏔᎵᏁ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᎨᏂᏪᏎᏗᏱ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ.
191219.english: And the sound of a trumpet, and the voice of words; which voice they that heard intreated that the word should not be spoken to them any more:
191219.phonetic: A-le a-tse-lu-gi u-no-yv-gv-i, a-le ka-ne-gv u-no-yv-gv-i; na-s-gi-no u-na-dv-ga-nv-hi u-ni-ta-yo-le ta-li-ne na-s-gi i-ge-ni-we-se-di-yi ni-ge-sv-na.

191220.cherokee: ᎥᏝᏰᏃ ᎣᏏᏳ ᏳᏂᏰᎸᏁ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎤᎵᏁᏨᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᎾᏜᏓᎢᏉ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏳᏃᏟᏍᏔ ᎣᏓᎸᎢ ᎠᏎ ᏅᏯ ᏣᎬᏂᏍᏙᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏥᏲᏍᏗᏉ ᎨᏒ ᏗᎬᏩᎶᏒᎯ.
191220.english: (For they could not endure that which was commanded, And if so much as a beast touch the mountain, it shall be stoned, or thrust through with a dart:
191220.phonetic: V-tla-ye-no o-si-yu yu-ni-ye-lv-ne nu-s-dv u-li-ne-tsv-i, Na-s-gi ga-na-dla-i-quo na-s-quo yu-no-tli-s-ta o-da-lv-i a-se nv-ya tsa-gv-ni-s-do-di ge-sv-i, a-le a-tsi-yo-s-di-quo ge-sv di-gv-wa-lo-sv-hi.

191221.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᎪᎲ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎤᎾᏰᎯᏍᏗᏳ ᎨᏒ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎼᏏ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ, ᎤᏣᏘ ᏥᎾᏰᏍᎦ ᎠᎴ ᎠᎩᎾᏫᎭ.
191221.english: And so terrible was the sight, that Moses said, I exceedingly fear and quake:)
191221.phonetic: A-le u-ni-go-hv u-tsa-ti u-na-ye-hi-s-di-yu ge-sv na-s-gi i-yu-s-di Mo-si hi-a nu-we-se-i, U-tsa-ti tsi-na-ye-s-ga a-le a-gi-na-wi-ha.

191222.cherokee: ᏌᏯᏂᏍᎩᏂ ᎣᏓᎸ ᎢᏥᎷᏨ, ᎠᎴ ᎬᏂᏛ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵ ᎦᏚᎲᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎡᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᎬᏎᎰᎲᏍᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏥᏅᏏᏓᏍᏗ ᏧᎾᏓᏡᎬᎢ,
191222.english: But ye are come unto mount Sion, and unto the city of the living God, the heavenly Jerusalem, and to an innumerable company of angels,
191222.phonetic: Sa-ya-ni-s-gi-ni o-da-lv i-tsi-lu-tsv, a-le gv-ni-dv U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li ga-du-hv-i, na-s-gi Tsi-lu-si-li-mi ga-lv-la-di e-hi, a-le di-gv-se-ho-hv-s-di ni-ge-sv-na ge-tsi-nv-si-da-s-di tsu-na-da-tlu-gv-i,

191223.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎾᎿᎭᏂᎦᏗᏳ ᎤᎾᏓᏟᏌᎲᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᎬᏱ ᎤᎾᏕᏅᎯ ᏧᎾᏁᎶᏗ ᎤᎾᏓᏡᎬᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ [ᏚᎾᏙᎥ] ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᏥᏕᎪᏪᎳ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᎿᎭᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏂᎦᏗᏳ ᏗᎫᎪᏓᏁᎯ [ᎡᎲᎢ,] ᎠᎴ ᎾᎿᎭᏧᎾᏓᏅᏙ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏘ ᏴᏫ ᎾᏂᏍᎦᏅᎾ ᎢᎨᎬᏁᎸᎯ [ᎠᏁᎲᎢ,]
191223.english: To the general assembly and church of the firstborn, which are written in heaven, and to God the Judge of all, and to the spirits of just men made perfect,
191223.phonetic: A-le na-hna ni-ga-di-yu u-na-da-tli-sa-hv-i, a-le i-gv-yi u-na-de-nv-hi tsu-na-ne-lo-di u-na-da-tlu-gv-i, na-s-gi [du-na-do-v] ga-lv-la-di tsi-de-go-we-la, a-le na-hna U-ne-la-nv-hi ni-ga-di-yu Di-gu-go-da-ne-hi [e-hv-i,] a-le na-hna tsu-na-da-nv-do u-na-da-nv-ti yv-wi na-ni-s-ga-nv-na i-ge-gv-ne-lv-hi [a-ne-hv-i,]

191224.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎾᎿᎭᏥᏌ [ᎡᎲᎢ] ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏰᎵ ᎠᎴᎲᏍᎩ ᎢᏤ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᏓᏠᎯᏍᏛᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᎿᎭᎩᎬ ᎠᏍᏚᏢᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏟ ᎢᏲᏍᏛ ᎠᏔᏲᎯᎯ ᏥᎩ ᎡᏍᎦᏉ ᎡᏈᎵ [ᎤᎩᎬ.]
191224.english: And to Jesus the mediator of the new covenant, and to the blood of sprinkling, that speaketh better things than that of Abel.
191224.phonetic: A-le na-hna Tsi-sa [e-hv-i] na-s-gi a-ye-li a-le-hv-s-gi i-tse ka-no-he-dv da-tlo-hi-s-dv-i, a-le na-hna gi-gv a-s-du-tlv-hi ge-sv-i, na-s-gi u-tli i-yo-s-dv a-ta-yo-hi-hi tsi-gi e-s-ga-quo E-qui-li [u-gi-gv.]

191225.cherokee: ᎢᏤᏯᏔᎮᏍᏗ ᏞᏍᏗᏉ ᎡᏥᏲᎢᏎᎸᎩ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᏥᎧᏁᎦ.  ᎢᏳᏰᏃ ᏄᎾᏓᏗᏫᏎᎸᎾ ᏱᎩ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎤᏂᏲᎢᏎᏛ ᎡᎶᎯ ᎤᏁᏨᎯ, ᎤᏟᎯᏳ ᎬᏩᏟᏍᏗ ᎢᎦᏓᏗᏫᏎᏗᏱ ᎢᏳᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏱᏁᏓᏛᏁᎸ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᏨᏗᎧᏁᎦ;
191225.english: See that ye refuse not him that speaketh. For if they escaped not who refused him that spake on earth, much more shall not we escape, if we turn away from him that speaketh from heaven:
191225.phonetic: I-tse-ya-ta-he-s-di tle-s-di-quo e-tsi-yo-i-se-lv-gi na-s-gi na tsi-ka-ne-ga.  I-yu-ye-no nu-na-da-di-wi-se-lv-na yi-gi na-s-gi na u-ni-yo-i-se-dv e-lo-hi u-ne-tsv-hi, u-tli-hi-yu gv-wa-tli-s-di i-ga-da-di-wi-se-di-yi i-yu-no i-yv-dv yi-ne-da-dv-ne-lv ga-lv-la-di tsv-di-ka-ne-ga;

191226.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎧᏁᎬ ᎾᎯᏳ ᎡᎶᎯ ᎤᏖᎸᏁᎢ; ᎠᏎᏃ ᎪᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏚᎢᏍᏔᏅ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒ, ᎠᏏ ᏌᏉ ᏅᏓᏥᏖᎸᏂ ᎥᏝ ᎡᎶᎯᏉ ᎤᏩᏒ, ᎦᎸᎶᎢᏍᎩᏂ ᎾᏍᏉ.
191226.english: Whose voice then shook the earth: but now he hath promised, saying, Yet once more I shake not the earth only, but also heaven.
191226.phonetic: Na-s-gi na ka-ne-gv na-hi-yu e-lo-hi u-te-lv-ne-i; a-se-no go-hi ge-sv u-du-i-s-ta-nv, hi-a nu-we-sv, A-si sa-quo nv-da-tsi-te-lv-ni v-tla e-lo-hi-quo u-wa-sv, ga-lv-lo-i-s-gi-ni na-s-quo.

191227.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏥᏂᎦᏪᎭ ᎠᏏ ᏌᏉᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎢᏗᎬᏁᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᏖᎸᏅᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎦᏛᎦ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᎪᏢᏅᎯᏉ ᎾᏍᎩᏯᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ Ꮎ ᏧᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏗᎬᏤᎸᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏒ ᎾᎿᎭᏉ ᏗᏍᏆᏂᎪᏙᏗ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ.
191227.english: And this word, Yet once more, signifieth the removing of those things that are shaken, as of things that are made, that those things which cannot be shaken may remain.
191227.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no hi-a tsi-ni-ga-we-ha A-si sa-quo-i, na-s-gi i-yv-dv i-di-gv-ne-di ge-sv na-s-gi di-te-lv-nv-hi ge-sv ga-dv-ga, na-s-gi di-go-tlv-nv-hi-quo na-s-gi-ya-i, na-s-gi-no na tsu-da-le-nv-dv di-gv-tse-lv-di ni-ge-sv-na ge-sv na-hna-quo di-s-qua-ni-go-do-di i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi.

191228.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏴ ᏗᎦᏓᏂᎸᏨᎯ ᏥᎩ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎪᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎬᏖᎸᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ, ᎬᏩᎦᏘᏯ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎢᎩᎮᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏛᏗᏍᎬ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎤᏰᎸᏗ ᏗᎨᎩᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᏱᎩ, ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎨᏒ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎦᎾᏰᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏔᎵ ᎢᎬᏗᏕᎬᎢ.
191228.english: Wherefore we receiving a kingdom which cannot be moved, let us have grace, whereby we may serve God acceptably with reverence and godly fear:
191228.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di na-s-gi a-yv di-ga-da-ni-lv-tsv-hi tsi-gi u-gv-wi-yu-hi u-tse-li-go-hi ge-sv na-s-gi gv-te-lv-di ni-ge-sv-na, gv-wa-ga-ti-ya u-da-do-li-s-di ge-sv i-gi-he-s-di, na-s-gi i-dv-di-s-gv U-ne-la-nv-hi o-s-dv u-ye-lv-di di-ge-gi-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di yi-gi, ga-lv-quo-di-yu ge-sv, a-le U-ne-la-nv-hi ga-na-ye-hi-s-di ge-sv ta-li i-gv-di-de-gv-i.

191229.english: For our God is a consuming fire.

191302.cherokee: ᏞᏍᏗ ᏱᏨᎨᏫᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᏂᏗᏣᏓᎦᏔᎲᎾ ᏗᏥᏍᏆᏂᎪᏙᏗᏱ; ᎩᎶᏰᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᎾᏛᏁᎲ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎠᏁᎯ ᎨᏥᏅᏏᏓᏍᏗ ᏧᏂᏍᏆᏂᎪᏔᏅᎯ ᎢᎩ.
191302.english: Be not forgetful to entertain strangers: for thereby some have entertained angels unawares.
191302.phonetic: Tle-s-di yi-tsv-ge-wi-s-ge-s-di ni-di-tsa-da-ga-ta-hv-na di-tsi-s-qua-ni-go-do-di-yi; gi-lo-ye-no na-s-gi na-na-dv-ne-hv ga-lv-la-di a-ne-hi ge-tsi-nv-si-da-s-di tsu-ni-s-qua-ni-go-ta-nv-hi i-gi.

191303.cherokee: ᏕᏣᏅᏓᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᏗᎨᎦᎸᎢᏛ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎢᏧᎳᎭ ᏥᏕᏣᎸᎣᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎤᏲ ᎢᎨᎬᎾᏕᎩ, ᎢᏨᏒᏰᏃ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎠᏰᎸ ᎢᏤᎭ.
191303.english: Remember them that are in bonds, as bound with them; and them which suffer adversity, as being yourselves also in the body.
191303.phonetic: De-tsa-nv-da-di-s-ge-s-di di-ge-ga-lv-i-dv ge-sv-i, na-s-gi-ya i-tsu-la-ha tsi-de-tsa-lv-o-i; a-le na-s-gi na u-yo i-ge-gv-na-de-gi, i-tsv-sv-ye-no na-s-quo a-ye-lv i-tse-ha.

191304.cherokee: ᏗᏨᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎾᏂᎥᏉ ᎠᏁᎲ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ ᏓᎾᏤᎲ ᎦᎳᏫᏎᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ; ᎤᏕᎵᏛᏍᎩᏂ ᏗᎾᏂᏏᎲᏍᎩ ᎠᎴ ᏗᎾᏓᏲᏁᎯ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏙᏓᎫᎪᏓᏁᎵ.
191304.english: Marriage is honourable in all, and the bed undefiled: but whoremongers and adulterers God will judge.
191304.phonetic: Di-tsv-s-di ge-sv na-ni-v-quo a-ne-hv ga-lv-quo-di-yu ge-se-s-di a-le da-na-tse-hv ga-la-wi-se-di ni-ge-sv-na ge-se-s-di; u-de-li-dv-s-gi-ni di-na-ni-si-hv-s-gi a-le di-na-da-yo-ne-hi U-ne-la-nv-hi do-da-gu-go-da-ne-li.

191305.cherokee: ᏕᏥᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎲ ᏞᏍᏗ ᏧᎬᏩᎶᏗ ᎠᎬᎥᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏳᎵᏠᏯᏍᏕᏍᏗ; ᎠᎴ ᏰᎵᏉ ᎢᏥᏰᎵᏎᏍᏗ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎢᏥᎲᎢ; ᎯᎠᏰᏃ ᎢᏳᏪᏛ ᎢᎩ, ᎥᏝ ᎢᎸᎯᏳ ᎤᏁᎳᎩ ᏴᎦᎬᏰᎵᏏ, ᎥᏝ ᎠᎴ ᏴᎦᎬᏴᏕᎩ.
191305.english: Let your conversation be without covetousness; and be content with such things as ye have: for he hath said, I will never leave thee, nor forsake thee.
191305.phonetic: De-tsi-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hv tle-s-di tsu-gv-wa-lo-di a-gv-v-hi-s-di ge-sv yu-li-tlo-ya-s-de-s-di; a-le ye-li-quo i-tsi-ye-li-se-s-di nu-s-dv go-hu-s-di i-tsi-hv-i; hi-a-ye-no i-yu-we-dv i-gi, V-tla i-lv-hi-yu u-ne-la-gi yv-ga-gv-ye-li-si, v-tla a-le yv-ga-gv-yv-de-gi.

191306.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏂᏗᎾᏰᏍᎬᎾ ᎯᎠ ᎢᎨᎩᏪᏍᏗ, ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎠᎩᏍᏕᎵᏍᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎥᏝ ᏴᎦᏥᏍᎦᏯ ᏴᏫ ᎢᎦᎬᏋᏁᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
191306.english: So that we may boldly say, The Lord is my helper, and I will not fear what man shall do unto me.
191306.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di ni-di-na-ye-s-gv-na hi-a i-ge-gi-we-s-di, U-gv-wi-yu-hi a-gi-s-de-li-s-gi, a-le v-tla yv-ga-tsi-s-ga-ya yv-wi i-ga-gv-quv-ne-di ge-sv-i.

191307.cherokee: ᏕᏣᏅᏓᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎨᏣᎦᏌᏯᏍᏗᏕᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎨᏥᎾᏄᎪᏫᏎᎸᎯ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎤᏃᎯᏳᏒ ᎢᏥᏍᏓᏩᏗᏎᏍᏗ, ᎣᏍᏛ ᎢᏣᏓᏅᏖᎯᏌᏛ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᏄᏍᏛ ᏭᎵᏰᎢᎶᎸ ᏚᏂᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎸᎢ,
191307.english: Remember them which have the rule over you, who have spoken unto you the word of God: whose faith follow, considering the end of their conversation.
191307.phonetic: De-tsa-nv-da-di-s-ge-s-di na-s-gi na ge-tsa-ga-sa-ya-s-di-de-gi, na-s-gi na U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li ka-no-he-dv ge-tsi-na-nu-go-wi-se-lv-hi; na-s-gi nu-s-dv u-no-hi-yu-sv i-tsi-s-da-wa-di-se-s-di, o-s-dv i-tsa-da-nv-te-hi-sa-dv ge-se-s-di nu-s-dv wu-li-ye-i-lo-lv du-ni-lv-wi-s-da-ne-lv-i,

191308.cherokee: ᏥᏌ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᏅᏩᏍᏗᏉ ᎤᏒᎯ ᏥᏄᏍᏛ, ᎠᎴ ᎪᎯ ᎢᎦ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎵᏍᏆᏗᏍᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ.
191308.english: Jesus Christ the same yesterday, and to day, and for ever.
191308.phonetic: Tsi-sa Ga-lo-ne-dv nv-wa-s-di-quo u-sv-hi tsi-nu-s-dv, a-le go-hi i-ga, a-le u-li-s-qua-di-s-di ni-ge-sv-na.

191309.cherokee: ᏞᏍᏗ ᎫᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᎠᎴ ᏂᏥᎦᏔᎲᎾ ᏗᏕᏲᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏱᏗᏣᏘᏂᏙᎮᏍᏗ; ᎣᏏᏳᏰᏃ ᎬᏩᎦᏘᏯ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏳᎵᏍᏓᏱᏗᏍᏗ ᎣᎾᏫ; ᎥᏝᏃ ᏄᏍᏛᏉ ᎥᎵᏍᏓᏴᎲᏍᎬᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎤᎾᎵᏍᏕᎸᏙᏗ ᏭᎵᏰᎢᎶᎸᎯ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏒ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎤᎾᎦᏌᏯᏍᏔᏅᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ.
191309.english: Be not carried about with divers and strange doctrines. For it is a good thing that the heart be established with grace; not with meats, which have not profited them that have been occupied therein.
191309.phonetic: Tle-s-di gu-da-le-nv-dv a-le ni-tsi-ga-ta-hv-na di-de-yo-di ge-sv yi-di-tsa-ti-ni-do-he-s-di; o-si-yu-ye-no gv-wa-ga-ti-ya u-da-do-li-s-di ge-sv yu-li-s-da-yi-di-s-di o-na-wi; v-tla-no nu-s-dv-quo v-li-s-da-yv-hv-s-gv-i, na-s-gi go-hu-s-di u-na-li-s-de-lv-do-di wu-li-ye-i-lo-lv-hi ni-ge-sv-na ge-sv na-s-gi i-yu-s-di u-na-ga-sa-ya-s-ta-nv-hi ge-sv-i.

191310.cherokee: ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎨᎳᏍᏗᏱ ᎢᎪᏢᎭ, ᎾᎿᎭᎥᏝ ᏱᏚᏳᎪᏗ ᎤᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗᏱ ᎦᎵᏦᏛᏉ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎠᎾᏛᏁᎯ ᏥᎩ.
191310.english: We have an altar, whereof they have no right to eat which serve the tabernacle.
191310.phonetic: A-tsi-lv--ge-la-s-di-yi i-go-tlv-ha, na-hna v-tla yi-du-yu-go-di u-na-li-s-da-yv-di-yi ga-li-tso-dv-quo go-hu-s-di a-na-dv-ne-hi tsi-gi.

191311.cherokee: ᎠᎴᏬ ᏅᎩ ᏗᏂᏅᏌᏗ ᎤᏂᎩᎬ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᏗᎨᏒ ᏥᏫᎦᏁᏨᏍᏗᏍᎨ ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎨᎶᎯ ᎠᏍᎦᏂ ᎠᎫᏴᏙᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᏂᏰᎸ ᎦᎵᏦᏛ ᏙᏱᏗᏢ ᏓᎾᎪᎲᏍᏗᏍᎨᎢ.
191311.english: For the bodies of those beasts, whose blood is brought into the sanctuary by the high priest for sin, are burned without the camp.
191311.phonetic: A-le-wo nv-gi di-ni-nv-sa-di u-ni-gi-gv ga-lv-quo-di-yu di-ge-sv tsi-wi-ga-ne-tsv-s-di-s-ge nu-gv-wi-yu-sv a-tsi-lv--ge-lo-hi a-s-ga-ni a-gu-yv-do-di, na-s-gi di-ni-ye-lv ga-li-tso-dv do-yi-di-tlv da-na-go-hv-s-di-s-ge-i.

191312.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏥᏌ ᎾᏍᏉ, ᎾᏍᎩ, ᎾᏂᏍᎦᏅᎾ ᎢᏧᏩᏁᏗᏱ ᏴᏫ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎤᎩᎬ ᎬᏗᏍᎬᎢ, ᎦᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ [ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ] ᏙᏱᏗᏢ ᎤᎩᎵᏲᏤᎢ.
191312.english: Wherefore Jesus also, that he might sanctify the people with his own blood, suffered without the gate.
191312.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di Tsi-sa na-s-quo, na-s-gi, na-ni-s-ga-nv-na i-tsu-wa-ne-di-yi yv-wi u-wa-sv u-gi-gv gv-di-s-gv-i, ga-lo-hi-s-di-yi [Tsi-lu-si-li-mi] do-yi-di-tlv u-gi-li-yo-tse-i.

191313.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎢᏕᎾ ᏪᏗᎷᏥ ᎦᎵᏦᏛ ᏙᏱᏗᏢ, ᎢᎦᏠᏯᏍᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎦᏰᏥᏐᏢᏗᏍᎬᎢ;
191313.english: Let us go forth therefore unto him without the camp, bearing his reproach.
191313.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di i-de-na we-di-lu-tsi ga-li-tso-dv do-yi-di-tlv, i-ga-tlo-ya-s-di-s-ge-s-di ga-ye-tsi-so-tlv-di-s-gv-i;

191314.cherokee: ᎠᏂᏰᏃ ᎥᏝ ᎠᏲᎩ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᏱᎩᏚᎭ, ᎢᎩᏲᎭᏍᎩᏂ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎤᎵᏴᎢᎶᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
191314.english: For here have we no continuing city, but we seek one to come.
191314.phonetic: A-ni-ye-no v-tla a-yo-gi ni-ge-sv-na yi-gi-du-ha, i-gi-yo-ha-s-gi-ni na-s-gi i-yu-s-di u-li-yv-i-lo-hi-s-di ge-sv-i.

191315.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏩᏂᏌᏛ ᏂᎪᎯᎸ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎦᎸᏉᏙᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏓᎵᏍᎪᎸᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏕᎩᎭᎦᎸ ᏧᎾᏄᎪᏫᏒᎯ ᎢᏓᎵᏍᎪᎸᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏕᎤᏙᎥ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏂᏛᏁᎲ ᎡᏓᎵᎡᎵᏤᎲᎢ.
191315.english: By him therefore let us offer the sacrifice of praise to God continually, that is, the fruit of our lips giving thanks to his name.
191315.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di, na-s-gi i-yu-wa-ni-sa-dv ni-go-hi-lv U-ne-la-nv-hi ga-lv-quo-do-di ge-sv i-da-li-s-go-lv-di-s-ge-s-di, na-s-gi de-gi-ha-ga-lv tsu-na-nu-go-wi-sv-hi i-da-li-s-go-lv-di-s-ge-s-di, na-s-gi de-u-do-v gv-ni-ge-sv ni-dv-ne-hv e-da-li-e-li-tse-hv-i.

191316.cherokee: ᎣᏍᏛᏍᎩᏂ ᏗᏥᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗᏱ ᎠᎴ ᎢᏣᏓᏁᏗᏱ ᏞᏍᏗ ᎢᏨᎨᏫᏒᎩ; ᎾᏍᎩᏰᏃ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏗᎵᏍᎪᎸᏙᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎣᏏᏳ ᎤᏰᎸᎭ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ.
191316.english: But to do good and to communicate forget not: for with such sacrifices God is well pleased.
191316.phonetic: O-s-dv-s-gi-ni di-tsi-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di-yi a-le i-tsa-da-ne-di-yi tle-s-di i-tsv-ge-wi-sv-gi; na-s-gi-ye-no i-yu-s-di di-li-s-go-lv-do-di ge-sv o-si-yu u-ye-lv-ha U-ne-la-nv-hi.

191317.cherokee: ᏕᏦᎯᏳᏎᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎨᏣᎦᏌᏯᏍᏗᏕᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏕᏣᏓᏲᏍᎨᏍᏗᏉ; ᏗᏣᏓᏅᏙᏰᏃ ᏚᎾᎦᏌᏯᏍᏗᏕᎦ, ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎢᏳᎾᏛᏁᏗ ᏥᎩ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᎢᏳᏅᏁᏗ ᏥᎨᏐ ᏄᎾᏛᏁᎵᏙᎸᎢ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᎾᎵᎮᎵᎬ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᎢᎬᏩᏅᏁᏗ ᏱᎩ, ᎥᏝᏃ ᎤᏲᏉ ᏚᎾᏓᏅᏛᎢ; ᎾᏍᎩᏰᏃ ᎥᏝ ᎨᏣᎵᏍᏕᎸᏙᏗ ᏱᎩ.
191317.english: Obey them that have the rule over you, and submit yourselves: for they watch for your souls, as they that must give account, that they may do it with joy, and not with grief: for that is unprofitable for you.
191317.phonetic: De-tso-hi-yu-se-s-di na-s-gi na ge-tsa-ga-sa-ya-s-di-de-gi, a-le de-tsa-da-yo-s-ge-s-di-quo; di-tsa-da-nv-do-ye-no du-na-ga-sa-ya-s-di-de-ga, na-s-gi-ya i-yu-na-dv-ne-di tsi-gi na-s-gi na gv-ni-ge-sv i-yu-nv-ne-di tsi-ge-so nu-na-dv-ne-li-do-lv-i; na-s-gi a-na-li-he-li-gv gv-ni-ge-sv i-gv-wa-nv-ne-di yi-gi, v-tla-no u-yo-quo du-na-da-nv-dv-i; na-s-gi-ye-no v-tla ge-tsa-li-s-de-lv-do-di yi-gi.

191318.cherokee: ᎢᏣᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗᏍᎬ ᎠᏴ ᏍᎩᏁᎢᏍᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ; ᎣᎪᎯᏳᎭᏰᏃ ᎣᏏᏳ ᏂᏙᎬᏅ ᏦᎦᏓᏅᏙ, ᎠᎴ ᎣᎦᏚᎵᎭ ᏂᎦᎥᏉ ᏃᏣᏛᏁᎵᏙᎲ ᏱᏍᏛ ᎢᏲᎦᏛᏁᏗᏱ.
191318.english: Pray for us: for we trust we have a good conscience, in all things willing to live honestly.
191318.phonetic: I-tsa-da-do-li-s-di-s-gv a-yv s-gi-ne-i-s-di-s-ge-s-di; o-go-hi-yu-ha-ye-no o-si-yu ni-do-gv-nv tso-ga-da-nv-do, a-le o-ga-du-li-ha ni-ga-v-quo no-tsa-dv-ne-li-do-hv yi-s-dv i-yo-ga-dv-ne-di-yi.

191319.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏍᎩᏂᏃᏅ ᎢᏣᏛᏁᏗᏱ ᎤᏟ ᎢᎦᎢ ᎢᏨᏍᏗᏰᏗᎭ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏞᎦᎾᎨ ᏗᏤᎲ ᏔᎵᏁ ᏮᏆᏘᏃᎯᏍᏗᏱ.
191319.english: But I beseech you the rather to do this, that I may be restored to you the sooner.
191319.phonetic: Hi-a-s-gi-ni-no-nv i-tsa-dv-ne-di-yi u-tli i-ga-i i-tsv-s-di-ye-di-ha, na-s-gi tle-ga-na-ge di-tse-hv ta-li-ne wv-qua-ti-no-hi-s-di-yi.

191320.cherokee: ᎤᏁᎳᏅᏗᏃ ᏅᏩᏙᎯᏯᏛ ᎠᏓᏁᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᎦᏤᎵ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏥᏌ ᎤᏲᎱᏒ ᏔᎵᏁ ᏧᎴᏔᏅᎯ ᏥᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎠᏥᎸᏉᏗ ᎠᏫ-ᏗᎦᏘᏯ ᎢᏯᎬᏁᎸᎯ, ᎬᏔᏅᎯ ᎩᎬ, ᎠᏲᎩ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᏓᏠᎯᏍᏛ ᎠᏍᏓᏱᏗᏍᎩ,
191320.english: Now the God of peace, that brought again from the dead our Lord Jesus, that great shepherd of the sheep, through the blood of the everlasting covenant,
191320.phonetic: U-ne-la-nv-di-no nv-wa-do-hi-ya-dv a-da-ne-hi, na-s-gi i-ga-tse-li U-gv-wi-yu-hi Tsi-sa u-yo-hu-sv ta-li-ne tsu-le-ta-nv-hi tsi-gi, na-s-gi na a-tsi-lv-quo-di A-wi--di-ga-ti-ya i-ya-gv-ne-lv-hi, gv-ta-nv-hi gi-gv, a-yo-gi ni-ge-sv-na ka-no-he-dv da-tlo-hi-s-dv a-s-da-yi-di-s-gi,

191321.cherokee: ᏂᏥᎪᎸᎾ ᎢᏨᏁᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᏂᎦᎥ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏗᎦᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᎨᏥᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎣᏏᏳ ᎤᏰᎸᏗ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎤᏰᎸᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏣᏛᏁᏗᏱ ᏂᏨᏁᎲ ᏥᏌ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᎢᏳᏩᏂᏌᏛ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏥᎸᏉᏙᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᏂᎪᎯᎸ ᎠᎴ ᏂᎪᎯᎸᎢ.  ᎠᎺᏅ.
191321.english: Make you perfect in every good work to do his will, working in you that which is wellpleasing in his sight, through Jesus Christ; to whom be glory for ever and ever. Amen.
191321.phonetic: Ni-tsi-go-lv-na i-tsv-ne-di ge-se-s-di, ni-ga-v o-s-dv di-ga-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di ge-sv na-s-gi di-ge-tsi-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di o-si-yu u-ye-lv-di i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi, na-s-gi o-s-dv u-ye-lv-di ge-sv i-tsa-dv-ne-di-yi ni-tsv-ne-hv Tsi-sa Ga-lo-ne-dv i-yu-wa-ni-sa-dv; na-s-gi a-tsi-lv-quo-do-di ge-se-s-di ni-go-hi-lv a-le ni-go-hi-lv-i.  A-me-nv.

191322.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎢᏨᏍᏗᏰᏗᎭ, ᎢᏓᎵᏅᏟ, ᎤᏓᏅᏘ ᏗᏣᏓᏂᎸᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᎢᏨᏬᏁᏗᏍᎬᎢ; ᎢᎸᏍᎩᏉᏰᏃ ᎢᎧᏁᏨᎯ ᎢᏨᏬᏁᏓ ᎠᏂ ᎪᏪᎵᎯ.
191322.english: And I beseech you, brethren, suffer the word of exhortation: for I have written a letter unto you in few words.
191322.phonetic: A-le i-tsv-s-di-ye-di-ha, i-da-li-nv-tli, u-da-nv-ti di-tsa-da-ni-lv-i-s-di-yi i-tsv-wo-ne-di-s-gv-i; i-lv-s-gi-quo-ye-no i-ka-ne-tsv-hi i-tsv-wo-ne-da a-ni go-we-li-hi.

191323.cherokee: ᎢᏣᏅᏖᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᎹᏗ ᎢᏓᎵᏅᏟ ᎤᏁᎳᏛᎢ; ᎢᏳᏃ ᏂᎪᎯᎸᎾ ᎢᎦᎷᏨᎭ ᎢᏨᎪᏩᏛᏗ ᎢᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎣᏍᏕᎮᏍᏗ.
191323.english: Know ye that our brother Timothy is set at liberty; with whom, if he come shortly, I will see you.
191323.phonetic: I-tsa-nv-te-s-di na-s-gi Di-ma-di i-da-li-nv-tli u-ne-la-dv-i; i-yu-no ni-go-hi-lv-na i-ga-lu-tsv-ha i-tsv-go-wa-dv-di i-ge-se-s-di na-s-gi o-s-de-he-s-di.

191324.cherokee: ᏕᏥᏲᎵᎸᎭ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎨᏣᎦᏌᏯᏍᏗᏕᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏘ.  ᎢᏓᎵ ᎠᏁᎯ ᏫᎨᏥᏲᎵᎦ.
191324.english: Salute all them that have the rule over you, and all the saints. They of Italy salute you.
191324.phonetic: De-tsi-yo-li-lv-ha ni-ga-dv ge-tsa-ga-sa-ya-s-di-de-gi, a-le ni-ga-dv u-na-da-nv-ti.  I-da-li a-ne-hi wi-ge-tsi-yo-li-ga.

191325.cherokee: ᎬᏩᎦᏘᏯ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏕᏥᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎮᏍᏗ ᏂᏥᎥᎢ.  ᎡᎺᏅ.
191325.english: Grace be with you all. Amen.
191325.phonetic: Gv-wa-ga-ti-ya u-da-do-li-s-di ge-sv de-tsi-ka-hna-wa-di-do-he-s-di ni-tsi-v-i.  E-me-nv.
